dtcs.description: A5-B19A3/A5: Short in Front Active Headrest Squib RH Circuit
A5 - Open in Front Active Headrest Squib RH Circuit
A5 - Short to GRD in Front Active Headrest Squib RH Circuit

A5 - Short to B+ in Front Active Headrest Squib RH Circuit The front active headrest squib RH circuit consists of the center airbag sensor and the active headrest inflator RH. This circuit instructs the SRS to deploy when deployment conditions are met. These DTCs are recorded when a malfunction is detected in the front active headrest squib RH circuit. HINT For vehicles not equipped with active headrest inflators: Vehicles that are not equipped with active headrest inflators still contain active headrest inflator circuits and use substitution parts in replace of the active headrest inflators. If a malfunction occurs in this active headrest inflator circuit, DTC B19A0, B19A1, B19A2 or

steps.instruction: CHECK ACTIVE HEADREST INFLATOR RH (FRONT ACTIVE HEADREST SQUIB RH) (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Disconnect the connector from the active headrest inflator RH. (d) Connect the red wire side of SST (resistance: 2.1 Ohms) to connector E.

CHECK FRONT ACTIVE HEADREST SQUIB RH CIRCUIT (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Disconnect SST from connector E. (d) Disconnect the connectors from the center airbag sensor. (e) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (f) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (g) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: (h) Turn the engine switch off. (i) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (j) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: (k) Release the activation prevention mechanism built into connector B    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System. (l) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: Result A --  CHECK CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR B -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FRONT SEAT WIRE RH (a) Disconnect the floor wire connector from the rear seat wire. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (d) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: (e) Turn the engine switch off. (f) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (g) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: (h) Release the activation prevention mechanism built into connector D    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System. (i) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE FRONT SEAT WIRE RH OK --  REPLACE FLOOR WIRE

CHECK CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR (a) Connect the connectors to the active headrest inflator RH and the center airbag sensor. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. (d) Clear the DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (e) Turn the engine switch off. (f) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. (g) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. OK: DTC B19A0, B19A1, B19A2, B19A3 or A5 is not output. HINT Codes other than DTC B19A0, B19A1, B19A2, B19A3 and A5 may be output at this time, but they are not related to this check. NG --  REPLACE CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal OK --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System DTC A6

dtcs.description: 11-C1314/14: Open in Main Relay 1 Circuit

14 - Short in Main Relay 2 Circuit The ABS main relay (skid control ECU interior circuit) remains ON for approximately 2 minutes after the engine switch is turned off and the input of brake operation signals stops, and supplies power to the system to keep it ready to operate. NOTICE: When replacing the skid control ECU, perform initialization of linear solenoid valve and calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: INSPECT FUSE (ABS MAIN1, ABS MAIN2, ABS MAIN3, D-IG1-3, ECU-IG) (a) Remove the ABS MAIN1, ABS MAIN2, ABS MAIN3, D-IG1-3 and ECU-IG fuses. (b) Measure the resistance of the fuses. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE FUSE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK TERMINAL VOLTAGE (+BI1, +BI2, +BI3, +BI4) (a) Disconnect the A19 and A21 ECU connectors. (b) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (c) Check if the same DTCs are recorded    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. HINT Reinstall the sensors, connectors, etc. and restore the vehicle to its prior condition before rechecking for DTCs. dtcs.description: 11 - Electric Parking Brake System Malfunction The skid control ECU is connected to the electric parking brake ECU via CAN communication. If an electric parking brake system malfunction occurs, this code is output. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC FOR ELECTRIC PARKING BRAKE SYSTEM (a) Clear the DTC (Electric parking brake system)    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Parking Brake System/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Check the DTC (Electric parking brake system)    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Parking Brake System/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result B --  GO TO ELECTRIC PARKING BRAKE SYSTEM  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Parking Brake System/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting A --  END Open in Power Supply Relay Circuit - Electric Parking Brake System

dtcs.description: 11, C13A5/41, C13B0: Open in Power Supply Relay Circuit

Electric Parking Brake ECU Malfunction The electric parking brake ECU performs the lock/release operation of the parking brake based on signals from the electric parking brake switch, or instruction signals from other systems via CAN communication. NOTICE: If the electric parking brake ECU or electric parking brake actuator is replaced, perform the "Reset Memory" and "ACQUIRE TENSION SENSOR ZERO POINT" procedures See: Brakes and Traction Control/Parking Brake System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: REPLACE ELECTRIC PARKING BRAKE ECU (a) Replace electric parking brake ECU    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Parking Brake System/Parking Brake Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal. NEXT --  END Room Temperature Sensor Circuit - Air Conditioning System

dtcs.description: 11 - Room Temperature Sensor Circuit The room temperature sensor for the front seat is installed in the instrument panel to detect the room temperature and control the heater and air conditioner "AUTO" mode. The resistance of the room temperature sensor changes in accordance with the room temperature. As the temperature decreases, the resistance increases. As the temperature increases, the resistance decreases. The air conditioning amplifier applies voltage (5 V) to the room temperature sensor and reads voltage changes as the resistance of the room temperature sensor changes. This sensor also sends the appropriate signals to the air conditioning amplifier. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (ROOM TEMPERATURE SENSOR) (a) Use the Data List to check if the room temperature sensor is functioning properly. Air Conditioner Result B --  PROCEED TO NEXT CIRCUIT INSPECTION SHOWN IN PROBLEM SYMPTOMS TABLE  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Testing and Inspection/Symptom Related Diagnostic Procedures C --  REPLACE AIR CONDITIONING AMPLIFIER  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Module HVAC/Service and Repair/Removal A -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT ROOM TEMPERATURE SENSOR (a) Remove the room temperature sensor    See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Cabin Temperature Sensor / Switch/Service and Repair/Room Temperature Sensor (for Front Side)/Removal. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NOTICE: * Even slightly touching the sensor may change the resistance value. Be sure to hold the connector of the sensor. * When measuring, the sensor temperature must be the same as the ambient temperature. HINT As the temperature increases, the resistance decreases (see the graph). NG --  REPLACE ROOM TEMPERATURE SENSOR  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Cabin Temperature Sensor / Switch/Service and Repair/Room Temperature Sensor (for Front Side)/Removal OK -- Continue to next step.

dtcs.description: 11-C2115/15: Transmitter ID1 Operation Stop

15 - Transmitter ID5 Operation Stop The tire pressure warning valve and transmitters that are installed in the tire and wheel assemblies measure the tire pressures of the tires. The measured values are transmitted to the tire pressure warning antenna and receiver on the body as radio waves and then sent to the tire pressure warning ECU. The ECU compares the measured tire pressure values with the tire pressure threshold. When the measured tire pressure value is less than this threshold, the PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: PERFORM FORCED TRANSMISSION OF TRANSMITTER ID OF ALL WHEELS (a) Set the tire pressures to the specified values    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Service Precautions. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and the Techstream on. (d) Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Data List / (e) Check the values by referring to the table below. Tire Pressure Monitor HINT * *: It may take about 2 or 3 minutes until the values are displayed. If the values are not displayed after a few minutes, perform according to the inspection procedure for DTCs C2121/21 to C2125/25    See: C Code Charts/C2121. * The tire pressure information reception condition can also be confirmed through the multi-information display's tire pressure display screen. (f) Rapidly reduce the tire pressure for each tire at least 40 kPa (0.4 kgf/cm2, 5.8 psi) within 30 seconds. (g) Check that each tire pressure data displayed on the Techstream screen has changed. OK: Each tire pressure data displayed on the Techstream screen changes to the value of the tire pressure. NOTICE: * It takes about 2 or 3 minutes to display the updated tire pressure data. * When the "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data has not changed, reset the tire pressure to the appropriate specified value and rotate the tire 90 to 270° (including the spare tire). Then rapidly release the tire pressure and recheck it. (h) After confirming that all of the tire pressure data displayed on the Techstream screen has changed, set the tire pressures to the appropriate specified values    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Service Precautions. HINT If the tire pressure data displayed on the Techstream screen has not changed after rechecking, inspect the other problem (malfunction in transmission or reception function)    See: C Code Charts/C2121. NG --  GO TO TRANSMITTER AND RECEIVER INSPECTION PROCEDURE  See: C Code Charts/C2121 OK --  END Open in Main Relay 1 Circuit - Electronically Controlled Brake System

dtcs.description: 12 - Ambient Temperature Sensor Circuit The ambient temperature sensor is installed in the front part of the condenser to detect the ambient temperature and control the air conditioner "AUTO" mode. The sensor connected to the front light ECU (front controller) detects fluctuations in the ambient temperature. This data is used for controlling the room temperature. The sensor sends a signal to the air conditioning amplifier via the front light ECU (front controller). The resistance of the ambient temperature sensor changes in accordance with the ambient temperature. As the temperature decreases, the resistance increases. As the temperature increases, the resistance decreases. The front light ECU (front controller) applies voltage (5 V) to the ambient temperature sensor and reads voltage changes as the resistance of the ambient temperature sensor changes. The front light ECU (front controller) sends the read signal to the air conditioning amplifier via the CAN communication line. HINT This circuit uses the CAN communication line. Before performing the inspection, check that the CAN communication system is functioning normally. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (AMBIENT TEMPERATURE SENSOR) (a) Use the Data List to check if the ambient temperature sensor is functioning properly. Air Conditioner OK: The display is as specified in the normal condition. NOTICE: After the engine switch is turned off, the HDD navigation system requires approximately 6 minutes to record various types of memory and settings. As a result, after turning the engine switch off, wait 6 minutes or more before disconnecting the cable from the negative (-) battery terminal. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK FRONT AIRBAG SENSOR RH (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Interchange the front airbag sensor LH with RH and connect the connectors to them. (d) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (e) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. (f) Clear the DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (g) Turn the engine switch off. (h) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. (i) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result HINT Codes other than DTC B1610/13 and B1615/14 may be output at this time, but they are not related to this check. dtcs.description: 13 - Evaporator Temperature Sensor Circuit The No. 1 cooler thermistor (evaporator temperature sensor) is installed on the evaporator in the air conditioner unit to detect the cooled air temperature that has passed through the evaporator and control the air conditioning. It sends appropriate signals to the air conditioning amplifier. The resistance of the No. 1 cooler thermistor (evaporator temperature sensor) changes in accordance with the cooled air temperature that has passed through the evaporator. As the temperature decreases, the resistance increases. As the temperature increases, the resistance decreases. The air conditioning amplifier applies voltage (5 V) to the No. 1 cooler thermistor (evaporator temperature sensor) and reads voltage changes as the resistance of the No. 1 cooler thermistor (evaporator temperature sensor) changes. This sensor is used for frost prevention. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (EVAPORATOR FIN THERMISTOR) (a) Use the Data List to check if the evaporator fin thermistor is functioning properly. dtcs.description: 18 - Exhaust Gas Sensor Circuit The exhaust gas sensor, which is installed on the front of the condenser, automatically changes to FRESH, FRESH / RECIRCULATION, or RECIRCULATION. The exhaust gas sensor detects CO and HC in the emission gas and transmits signals to the front light ECU (front controller). The front light ECU (front controller) sends the read signal to the air conditioning amplifier via CAN communication line. HINT This circuit uses the CAN communication line. Before performing the inspection, check that the CAN communication system is functioning normally. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 19 - Rear Room Temperature Sensor Circuit The rear room temperature sensor LH is installed in the rear door trim board LH to detect the rear room temperature and control the heater and air conditioner "AUTO" mode. The resistance of the rear room temperature sensor changes in accordance with the rear room temperature for LH seat. As the temperature decreases, the resistance increases. As the temperature increases, the resistance decreases. The rear door ECU LH sends the read signal to the air conditioning amplifier via the CAN communication line. The rear door ECU LH applies voltage (5 V) to the rear room temperature sensor and reads voltage changes as the resistance of the rear room temperature sensor changes. This sensor also sends the appropriate signals to the rear door ECU LH. HINT This circuit uses the CAN communication line. Before performing the inspection, check that the CAN communication system is functioning normally. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 21-C2125/25, C2181/81-C2185/85: No Signal from Transmitter ID1 in Main Mode

85 - Transmitter ID5 not Received (Test Mode DTC) The tire pressure warning valve and transmitters that are installed in the tire and wheel assemblies measure the tire pressure of the tires. The measured values are transmitted to the tire pressure warning antenna and receiver on the body as radio waves and then sent to the tire pressure warning ECU. The ECU compares the measured tire pressure values with the tire pressure threshold. When the measured tire pressure value is less than this threshold, the NOTICE: * When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and write them down before removal. * It is necessary to perform initialization See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization after registration See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter has been replaced. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: READ OUTPUT DTC (DTC C2179/79) (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and the Techstream on. (d) Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Trouble Codes / (e) Read DTCs. Result NOTICE: When DTC C2179/79 is output, troubleshoot that DTC first. Then troubleshoot DTCs C2121/21 to C2125/25. B --  GO TO DTC C2179/79  See: C Code Charts/C2179 A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FREQUENCY RECEIVING CONDITION (a) Check for the conditions described below: (1) Facilities or devices that use similar radio frequencies are located in the vicinity of the vehicle. HINT If the vehicle is located in areas described above, the tire pressure warning light may turn on only in a particular area. (2) Devices using similar radio frequencies are used in the vehicle. OK: Facilities or devices that use similar radio frequencies are not located around or in the vehicle. HINT Radio frequencies may be interrupted due to the surroundings, or devices installed by the user. NG --  CHECK IF ANY DEVICE IS INSTALLED BY USER OK -- Continue to next step.

(b) Turn the engine switch off. (c) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (d) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and the Techstream on. (e) Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Data List / (f) Check the values by referring to the table below. Tire Pressure Monitor HINT * *: It may take about 2 or 3 minutes until the values are displayed. * The tire pressure information reception condition can also be confirmed through the multi-information display's tire pressure display screen. (g) Rapidly reduce the tire pressure for each wheel at least 40 kPa (0.4 kgf/cm2, 5.8 psi) within 30 seconds. (h) Check the Data List. NOTICE: * It takes about 2 or 3 minutes to display the updated tire pressure data. * When "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data has not changed, reset the tire pressure to the appropriate specified value and rotate the tire 90 to 270°. Then rapidly release the tire pressure and recheck it. * Record the "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data transmitter ID that corresponds to each tire. (i) After confirming that the "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data for one tire (ID1 to ID5) has changed, repeat this procedure for each tire. Identify the transmitter that corresponds to the DTC. Result B --  CHECK TRANSMITTER ID C --  END A -- Continue to next step.

dtcs.description: 21 or B1625/22 is recorded when a malfunction is detected in the side airbag sensor LH or RH circuit. for DTC B1622 and B1627: The circuit for the side collision sensor RH or LH (to determine deployment of the front seat side airbag RH or LH, rear seat side airbag RH or LH, and curtain shield airbag RH or LH) is composed of the center airbag sensor, side airbag sensor RH or LH, and rear airbag sensor RH or LH. The side airbag sensor RH or LH, and rear airbag sensor RH or LH detect impacts to the vehicle and send signals to the center airbag sensor to determine if the airbag should be deployed.

82 - Lost communication with Side Airbag Sensor LH for DTC B1620 and B1625: The side airbag sensor LH and RH consists of the safing sensor, the diagnostic circuit, the lateral deceleration sensor, etc. If the center airbag sensor receives signals from the lateral deceleration sensor, it determines whether the SRS should be activated.

or B1627 is recorded when a malfunction is detected in the circuit for the side collision sensor RH or LH (to determine deployment of the front seat side airbag RH or LH, rear seat side airbag RH or LH, and curtain shield airbag RH or LH). NOTICE: After the engine switch is turned off, the HDD navigation system requires approximately 6 minutes to record various types of memory and settings. As a result, after turning the engine switch off, wait 6 minutes or more before disconnecting the cable from the negative (-) battery terminal. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 31, C1316/32, C1352/21-C1359/28: SMC1 Changeover Solenoid Malfunction

28 - Rear Decreasing Pressure Solenoid LH Malfunction Each solenoid adjusts pressure which affects each wheel cylinder according to signals from the skid control ECU and controls the vehicle. The master cut solenoid (SMC 1/2) is closed and blocks the master cylinder pressure from the electronically controlled brake system control pressure when the system is normal. The master cut solenoid is open and sends the master cylinder fluid pressure to the non-assisted brake wheel cylinders during the fail-safe operation when the system is malfunctioning. NOTICE: When replacing the skid control ECU or brake actuator, perform initialization of linear solenoid valve and calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 21 - Solar Sensor Circuit (Passenger Side) The solar sensor, which is installed on the upper side of the instrument panel, detects sunlight and controls the air conditioning in AUTO mode. The output current from the solar sensor varies according to the amount of sunlight. When the sunlight increases, the output current increases. As the sunlight decreases, the output current decreases. The air conditioning amplifier detects output current from the solar sensor. HINT If DTC B1244 is output at the same time, troubleshoot DTC B1244 first. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 22 - Engine / Power Switch Malfunction When the engine switch is turned on (IG), the IG operation power supply is output from the main body ECU. Power is then supplied from the luggage room junction block's RR-IG1 relay, which turns the RR-IG1 relay ON. Power is then supplied to the parking brake ECU. NOTICE: * If the electric parking brake ECU or electric parking brake actuator is replaced, perform the "Reset Memory" and "ACQUIRE TENSION SENSOR ZERO POINT" procedures See: Brakes and Traction Control/Parking Brake System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. * Before disconnecting connectors or fuses, turn the engine switch off and wait 20 seconds or more. dtcs.description: 24 - Rear Airbag Sensor LH Circuit Malfunction The rear airbag sensor LH or RH consists of the safing sensor, the diagnostic circuit, the lateral deceleration sensor, etc. If the center airbag sensor receives signals from the lateral deceleration sensor, it determines whether the SRS should be activated.

23 - Pressure Sensor Circuit This DTC is output when refrigerant pressure is extremely low (0.19 MPa (2.0 kgf/cm2, 28 psi) or less) or extremely high (3.14 MPa (32.0 kgf/cm2, 455 psi) or more). The pressure sensor, which is installed on the pipe of the high pressure side to detect refrigerant pressure, outputs the refrigerant pressure signal to the front light ECU (front controller). The front light ECU (front controller) sends the read signal to the air conditioning amplifier via the CAN communication line. The air conditioning amplifier converts the signals to pressure according to the sensor characteristics to control the compressor. HINT * Be sure to check the refrigerant volume first when this DTC is output because this DTC can also be output if there is no refrigerant in the cycle. * This circuit uses the CAN communication line. Before performing the inspection, check that the CAN communication system is functioning normally. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 24 - Solar Sensor Circuit (Driver Side) The solar sensor, which is installed on the upper side of the instrument panel, detects sunlight and controls the air conditioning in AUTO mode. The output current from the solar sensor varies according to the amount of sunlight. When the sunlight increases, the output current increases. As the sunlight decreases, the output current decreases. The A/C amplifier detects output current from the solar sensor. HINT If DTC B1244 is output at the same time, troubleshoot DTC B1244 first. PROCEDURE

If the tire pressure warning light does not turn off after a little while, IDs may be incorrectly registered. steps.instruction: IDENTIFY TRANSMITTER ID NOT RECEIVED (a) Set the tire pressures to the appropriate specified values    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Service Precautions. Tire Pressure Monitor HINT * *: It may take about 2 or 3 minutes until the values are displayed. * The tire pressure information reception condition can also be confirmed through the multi-information display's tire pressure display screen. (g) Rapidly reduce the tire pressure for each wheel at least 40 kPa (0.4 kgf/cm2, 5.8 psi) within 30 seconds. (h) Check the Data List. NOTICE: * It takes about 2 or 3 minutes to display the updated tire pressure data. * When "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data has not changed, reset the tire pressure to the appropriate specified value and rotate the tire 90 to 270°. Then rapidly release the tire pressure and recheck it. * Record the "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data transmitter ID that corresponds to each tire. (i) After confirming that the "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data for one tire (ID1 to ID5) has changed, repeat this procedure for each tire. Identify the transmitter ID not received. dtcs.description: 31, C13AB/33: Open or Short in Lock Switch Circuit

33 - Lock Switch Circuit When electric parking brake switch is pushed to the lock side, a lock request signal is output to parking brake ECU. HINT *: When battery voltage is 11 V NOTICE: * If the electric parking brake ECU or electric parking brake actuator is replaced, perform the "Reset Memory" and "ACQUIRE TENSION SENSOR ZERO POINT" procedures See: Brakes and Traction Control/Parking Brake System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. * Before disconnecting connectors or fuses, turn the engine switch off and wait 20 seconds or more. * The electric parking brake switch interior's linked double contacts may become unlinked (one is off, one is on) if the switch is halfway pushed/pulled. If this condition continues for 1 second or more, this DTC is output. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 31 - Center Airbag Sensor Assembly Malfunction The center airbag sensor assembly consists of a deceleration sensor, safing sensor, drive circuit, diagnosis circuit, ignition control, etc. If the center airbag sensor assembly receives signals from the deceleration sensor, it determines whether or not the SRS should be activated.

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC (a) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. HINT If any DTCs other than DTC B1000/31 are also output, troubleshoot those DTCs first. dtcs.description: 31, C0205/32, C1271/71, C1272/72: Front Speed Sensor RH Circuit

72 - Low Output Signal of Front Speed Sensor LH (Test Mode DTC) The speed sensor detects wheel speed and sends the appropriate signals to the ECU. These signals are used to control the brake control system. This speed sensor contains a sensor IC, which consists of 2 MREs (Magnetic Resistance Element). The speed sensor rotor, which consists of 48 sets of N and S poles that are arranged in a circle, is integrated with the inner race of the hub bearing. To detect the rotation direction, the output signals from the speed sensor are used to determine the relationship of the pulses that are generated by the 2 MREs. Upon receiving the speed sensor signal, the sensor IC outputs a forward or backward signal to the ECU. DTCs C1271/71 and C1272/72 are cleared when the speed sensor sends a vehicle speed signal or test mode ends. DTCs C1271/71 and C1272/72 are output only in test mode. HINT *

32 is for the front speed sensor LH. NOTICE: When replacing the skid control ECU, perform initialization of linear solenoid valve and calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Calibration. HINT Start the inspection from "CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR" when using the Techstream and start from "PERFORM TEST MODE" when not using the Techstream. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (MOMENTARY INTERRUPTION) (a) Using the Techstream, check for any momentary interruptions in the wire harness and connector corresponding to a DTC    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics/Electronically Controlled Brake System/Check for Intermittent Problems. ABS/VSC/TRAC Result HINT Perform the above inspection before removing the sensor and connector. B --  READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (FRONT SPEED SENSOR) C --  CHECK FRONT SPEED SENSOR INSTALLATION D --  CHECK FRONT SPEED SENSOR INSTALLATION A -- Continue to next step.

REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR (SKID CONTROL ECU - FRONT SPEED SENSOR) (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Repair or replace the harness or connector. (c) Check for any momentary interruption between the skid control ECU and the front speed sensor    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics/Electronically Controlled Brake System/Check for Intermittent Problems. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Clear the DTCs    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (c) Start the engine. (d) Drive the vehicle at a speed of 32 km/h (20 mph) or more for at least 60 seconds. (e) Check if the same DTC is recorded    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result HINT If troubleshooting has been carried out according to the Problem Symptoms Table, refer back to the table and proceed to the next step    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Symptom Related s/Electronically Controlled Brake System/Problem Symptoms Table. B --  END A -- Continue to next step.

READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (FRONT SPEED SENSOR) (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Start the engine. (d) Select the Data List mode on the Techstream    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Data List / Active Test. ABS/VSC/TRAC (e) Check that there is no difference between the speed value output from the speed sensor displayed on the Techstream and the speed value displayed on the speedometer when driving the vehicle. HINT Factors that affect the indicated vehicle speed include tire size, tire inflation, and tire wear. The speed indicated on the speedometer has an allowable margin of error. The margin of error can be tested using a speedometer tester (calibrated chassis dynamometer). For details about testing and the margin of error, see the reference chart    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Operation Check. OK: The speed value output from the speed sensor displayed on the Techstream is the same as the actual vehicle speed. ABS/VSC/TRAC (f) Check that the sensor signal conforms to the wheel rotation direction. OK: The correct wheel rotation direction is displayed. Result B --  CHECK FRONT SPEED SENSOR INSTALLATION C --  CHECK FRONT SPEED SENSOR INSTALLATION A -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM TEST MODE (SIGNAL CHECK) (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Perform the sensor check in test mode    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Test Mode Procedure. dtcs.description: 31 - Steering Angle Sensor Circuit Malfunction The steering angle sensor signal is sent to the skid control ECU via the CAN communication system. When there is a malfunction in the CAN communication system, it will be detected by the diagnosis function. HINT * When U0073/94, U0293/59, U0123/62, U0124/95, U0126/63 or U0130/68 is output together with C1231/31, inspect and repair the
possible_causes: When the speed sensor or the yaw rate sensor has trouble, DTCs for the steering angle sensor may be output even when the
steering angle sensor is normal. When DTCs for the speed sensor or yaw rate sensor are output together with other DTCs for the
steering angle sensor, inspect and repair the speed sensor and yaw rate sensor first, and then inspect and repair the steering angle
Start the inspection from "CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR" when using the Techstream and start from "CHECK DTC"

steps.instruction: CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (MOMENTARY INTERRUPTION) (a) Using the Techstream, check for any momentary interruption in the wire harness and connectors between the skid control ECU and the steering angle sensor    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics/Electronically Controlled Brake System/Check for Intermittent Problems. ABS/VSC/TRAC OK: There are no momentary interruptions. HINT Perform the above inspection before removing the sensor and connector. NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR (STEERING ANGLE SENSOR - SKID CONTROL ECU) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Turn the engine switch off. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG) again and check that no CAN communication system DTC is output. (d) Drive the vehicle at a speed of 35 km/h (24 mph), turn the steering wheel to the right and left, and check that no speed sensor and yaw rate sensor DTCs are output. Result HINT * If there is a malfunction in the speed sensor or the yaw rate sensor, an abnormal value may be output although the steering angle sensor is normal. * If the speed sensor and the yaw rate sensor DTCs are output simultaneously, repair the sensors and inspect the steering angle sensor. B --  GO TO CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/CAN Communication System/How to Proceed With C --  CHECK FOR CIRCUIT INDICATED BY OUTPUT CODE  See: Diagnostic Trouble Code Descriptions/Electronically Controlled Brake System A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK TERMINAL VOLTAGE (IG, BAT) (a) Remove the steering wheel and the column cover lower. (b) Disconnect the L21 sensor connector. (c) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: (d) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: HINT If troubleshooting has been carried out according to the "PROBLEM SYMPTOMS TABLE", refer back to the table and proceed to the next step    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Symptom Related Diagnostic Procedures/Electronically Controlled Brake System/Problem Symptoms Table. NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR (IG, BAT OR ESS CIRCUIT) OK --  REPLACE STEERING ANGLE SENSOR  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Steering Angle Sensor/Service and Repair/Removal
REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR (STEERING ANGLE SENSOR - SKID CONTROL ECU) NEXT -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Start the engine. (c) Drive the vehicle at a speed of 35 km/h (24 mph) or more, and turn the steering wheel to the right and left. (d) Check if the same DTC is recorded    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result HINT If troubleshooting has been carried out according to the "PROBLEM SYMPTOMS TABLE", refer back to the table and proceed to the next step    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Symptom Related Diagnostic Procedures/Electronically Controlled Brake System/Problem Symptoms Table. dtcs.description: 32, C13AC/34: Open or Short in Release Switch Circuit

34 - Release Switch Circuit When electric parking brake switch is pushed to the release side, a release request signal is output to parking brake ECU. HINT *: When battery voltage is 11 V NOTICE: * If the electric parking brake ECU or electric parking brake actuator is replaced, perform the "Reset Memory" and "ACQUIRE TENSION SENSOR ZERO POINT" procedures See: Brakes and Traction Control/Parking Brake System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. * Before disconnecting connectors or fuses, turn the engine switch off and wait 20 seconds or more. * The electric parking brake switch interior's linked double contacts may become unlinked (one is off, one is on) if the switch is halfway pushed/pulled. If this condition continues for 1 second or more, this DTC is output. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: INSPECT ELECTRIC PARKING BRAKE SWITCH (a) Remove the electric parking brake switch    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Parking Brake System/Electric Parking Brake Switch/Service and Repair/Removal. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. dtcs.description: 32 is recorded when a malfunction is detected in the occupant classification system circuit. NOTICE: After the engine switch is turned off, the HDD navigation system requires approximately 6 minutes to record various types of memory and settings. As a result, after turning the engine switch off, wait 6 minutes or more before disconnecting the cable from the negative (-) battery terminal. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK FOR DTC (OCCUPANT CLASSIFICATION ECU) (a) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 10 seconds. (b) Check for the DTCs of the occupant classification ECU    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. OK: DTC is not output. NG --  REPAIR CIRCUITS INDICATED BY OUTPUT DTCS  See: Diagnostic Trouble Code Descriptions/Occupant Classification System OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR DTC (CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR) (a) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. (b) Clear the DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (c) Turn the engine switch off. (d) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. (e) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. OK:

CHECK CONNECTION OF CONNECTORS (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Check that the connectors are properly connected to the center airbag sensor and the occupant classification ECU. OK: The connectors are properly connected. NG --  CONNECT CONNECTORS PROPERLY OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CONNECTORS (a) Disconnect the connectors from the center airbag sensor and the occupant classification ECU. (b) Check that the connectors (on the center airbag sensor side and occupant classification ECU side) are not damaged. OK: The connectors are not deformed or damaged. NG --  REPLACE HARNESS AND CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK OCCUPANT CLASSIFICATION SYSTEM CIRCUIT (a) Using a service wire, connect terminals 16 (FSR+) and 22 (FSR-) of connector B. (c) Disconnect the front seat wire RH connector from the floor wire. (d) Using a service wire, connect terminals 16 (FSR+) and 22 (FSR-) of connector B. (e) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: (f) Disconnect the service wire from connector B. (g) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: (h) Turn the engine switch off. dtcs.description: 97 - Acceleration Sensor Power Supply Voltage Malfunction The skid control ECU receives signals from the yaw rate and acceleration sensor via the CAN communication system. The yaw rate sensor has a built-in acceleration sensor and detects the vehicle's condition using 2 circuits (GL1, GL2). If there is trouble in the bus lines between the yaw rate and acceleration sensor and the CAN communication system, the DTC U0123/62 (malfunction in CAN communication with yaw rate sensor) and U0124/95 (malfunction in CAN communication with acceleration sensor) are output. These DTCs are also output when the calibration has not been completed. DTCs C0371/71 and C1279/79 can be deleted when the yaw rate and acceleration sensor sends a yaw rate and/or acceleration signal or the Test Mode ends. DTCs C0371/71 and C1279/79 are output only in the Test Mode. NOTICE: When replacing the yaw rate and acceleration sensor, perform zero point calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Calibration. HINT When U0123/62, U0124/95 or U0126/63 is output together with C1234/34, C1243/43, C1244/44, C1245/45 or C1381/97, inspect and repair the trouble areas indicated by U0123/62, U0124/95 or U0126/63 first. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 36, C1336/98: Zero Point Calibration of Yaw Rate Sensor Undone

98 - Zero Point Calibration of Acceleration Sensor Undone The skid control ECU receives signals from the yaw rate and acceleration sensor via the CAN communication system. The yaw rate sensor has the built-in acceleration sensor and detects the vehicle's condition using 2 circuits (GL1, GL2). If there is trouble in the bus lines between the yaw rate and acceleration sensor and the CAN communication system, the DTCs U0123/62 (yaw rate sensor communication trouble) and U0124/95 (acceleration sensor communication trouble) are output. The DTCs are also output when the calibration has not been completed. NOTICE: When replacing the skid control ECU, perform initialization of the linear solenoid valve and calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. HINT When U0123/62, U0124/95 or U0126/63 is output along with C1210/36 or C1336/98, inspect and repair the trouble areas indicated by
62, U0124/95 or U0126/63 first. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 41 - Air Mix Damper Control Servo Motor Circuit (Passenger Side) The air mix damper servo (for passenger side HOT) sends pulse signals to inform the air conditioning amplifier of the damper position. The air conditioning amplifier activates the motor (normal, reverse) based on the signals to move the air mix damper (for passenger side HOT) to any position. As a result the amount of air that has passed through the evaporator and is now passing through the heater core is adjusted, and the temperature of the air blowing toward the passenger side is controlled. HINT Confirm that no mechanical problem is present because this diagnostic code can be output when either a damper link or damper is mechanically locked. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (HOT AIR MIX SERVO MOTOR TARGET PULSE (for Passenger Side)) (a) Use the Data List to check if the servo motor is functioning properly. Air Conditioner OK: The display is as specified in the normal condition. Result B --  PROCEED TO NEXT CIRCUIT INSPECTION SHOWN IN PROBLEM SYMPTOMS TABLE  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Testing and Inspection/Symptom Related Diagnostic Procedures C --  REPLACE AIR CONDITIONING AMPLIFIER  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Module HVAC/Service and Repair/Removal A -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE AIR MIX SERVO MOTOR (for Passenger Side HOT) HINT Since the servo motor cannot be inspected while it is removed from the vehicle, replace the servo motor with a normal one and check that the condition returns to normal    See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Housing Assembly HVAC/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Air Conditioning Unit (for Front Side)/Disassembly. OK: Same problem does not occur. NG --  REPLACE AIR CONDITIONING HARNESS  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Housing Assembly HVAC/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Air Conditioning Unit (for Front Side)/Disassembly OK --  END Low Battery Positive Voltage - Electronically Controlled Brake System

dtcs.description: 41, C1242/42: Low Battery Positive Voltage

42 - Open in IG1 / IG2 Power Source Circuit These codes are memorized when the power source voltage for the skid control ECU drops or the voltage for the ABS MAIN1 relay and ABS MAIN2 relay operation drops. The ABS MAIN1 relay and ABS MAIN2 relay are in the skid control ECU. HINT

57 - Accumulator Low Pressure The accumulator pressure sensor is built into the actuator and detects the accumulator pressure. The skid control ECU turns on the brake warning light / yellow (minor malfunction) and sounds the skid control buzzer if it senses a decrease in the accumulator pressure. HINT DTCs may be output if the accumulator pressure drops due to frequent braking (this is not a malfunction). NOTICE: When replacing the skid control ECU or brake actuator, perform initialization of linear solenoid valve and calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. HINT When C1202/68, C1241/41, C1252/52, C1253/53 or C1391/69 are output together with C1256/57, inspect and repair the trouble areas indicated by C1202/68, C1241/41, C1252/52, C1253/53 or C1391/69 first. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: INSPECT BATTERY (a) Check the battery voltage. Standard voltage: 11 to 14 V NG --  INSPECT CHARGING SYSTEM  See: Starting and Charging/Charging System/Testing and Inspection OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Turn the engine switch off. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (d) Check that the DTCs (electronically controlled brake system and SFI system) are recorded. Result B --  GO TO SFI SYSTEM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How to Proceed with Troubleshooting C --  REPAIR CIRCUIT INDICATED BY OUTPUT CODE  See: Diagnostic Trouble Code Descriptions/Electronically Controlled Brake System A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK TERMINAL VOLTAGE (IG1, IG2, +BI1, +BI2, +BI3, +BI4) (a) Disconnect the A19 and A21 ECU connectors. (b) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR (IG OR BI CIRCUIT) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (GND CIRCUIT) (a) Disconnect the A19 and A21 ECU connectors. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR (GND CIRCUIT) OK -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (c) After 2 minutes or more have elapsed, drive the vehicle 5 km/h (3 mph) or more for 20 seconds. (d) Check if the same DTCs are recorded    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result HINT If troubleshooting has been carried out according to the "PROBLEM SYMPTOMS TABLE", refer back to the table and proceed to the next step    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Symptom Related Diagnostic Procedures/Electronically Controlled Brake System/Problem Symptoms Table. B --  REPLACE SKID CONTROL ECU  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Electronic Brake Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal A --  END Transmitter ID1 Error - Tire Pressure Monitoring

dtcs.description: 41-C2145/45: Transmitter ID1 Error

45 - Transmitter ID5 Error When the internal circuit of a tire pressure warning valve and transmitter is malfunctioning, one of these DTCs is output. HINT It is necessary to perform the procedure to identify the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter that is malfunctioning because it cannot be identified by the output DTC. NOTICE: * When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and write them down before removal. * It is necessary to perform initialization See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization after registration See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter has been replaced. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: IDENTIFY TRANSMITTER CORRESPONDING TO DTC (a) Set the tire pressures to the specified values    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Service Precautions. (b) Turn the engine switch off. (c) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (d) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and the Techstream on. (e) Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Data List / (f) Display the "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data for each wheel using the Techstream. (g) Rapidly reduce the tire pressure for each wheel at least 40 kPa (0.4 kgf/cm2, 5.8 psi) within 30 seconds. If "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data displayed on the Techstream (ID1 to ID5) does not change, the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter corresponding to the unchanged "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data was the cause of the output DTC. HINT * Identify the malfunctioning tire pressure warning valve and transmitter by repeatedly decreasing the tire pressure for each tire. * Record which "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data (ID1 to ID5) corresponds to which tire. (h) Check the Data List. Tire Pressure Monitor HINT * *: It may take about 2 or 3 minutes until the values are displayed. If the values are not displayed after a few minutes, perform according to the inspection procedure for DTCs C2121/21 to C2125/25    See: C Code Charts/C2121. * The tire pressure information reception condition can also be confirmed through the multi-information display's tire pressure display screen. Result NOTICE: * It takes about 2 or 3 minutes to display the update tire pressure data. * When "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data has not changed, reset the tire pressure to the appropriate specified value and rotate the tire 90 to 270°. Then forcibly transmit the transmitter ID and recheck. * Record the transmitter ID and position of transmitters that are normal. (i) When an "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data value (one of ID1 to ID5) has changed, repeat this procedure for each tire. (j) When all of the "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data (ID1 to ID5) has changed, identify the malfunctioning tire pressure warning valve and transmitter using the recorded ID numbers and output DTC. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING VALVE AND TRANSMITTER (a) Replace the identified tire pressure warning valve and transmitter with a new one    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Sensor/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal. HINT * Before installing a new tire pressure warning valve and transmitter, read and write down its transmitter ID. * The IDs for the tire pressure warning valve and transmitters that are not replaced should be checked using the Techstream and recorded. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM REGISTRATION OF TRANSMITTER ID (a) Register the transmitter IDs for all wheels    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM INITIALIZATION (a) Perform initialization    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CONFIRM TIRE INFLATION PRESSURE (DATA LIST)   See: C Code Charts/C2121 B --  END A --  REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Module/Service and Repair/Removal VGRS Control System Malfunction - Electronically Controlled Brake System

dtcs.description: 41 - VGRS Control System Malfunction The skid control ECU communicates with the steering control ECU via CAN communication. If a malfunction in the VGRS system is detected, cooperative control of the steering is prohibited by the fail-safe function. NOTICE: When replacing the skid control ECU, perform initialization of linear solenoid valve and calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. PROCEDURE /Variable Gear Ratio Steering System/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting A -- END Low Battery Positive Voltage - Electronically Controlled Brake System

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC FOR VGRS SYSTEM (a) Clear the DTC    See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Steering Column/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Power Tilt and Power Telescopic Steering Column System. (b) Check for the DTC    See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Steering Column/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Power Tilt and Power Telescopic Steering Column System. Result B --  GO TO VGRS SYSTEM  See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic

dtcs.description: 42 - Open or Short in Circuit of Motor The parking brake cable tightening/loosening operation is performed by the parking brake actuator's motor. NOTICE: * If the electric parking brake ECU or electric parking brake actuator is replaced, perform the "Reset Memory" and "ACQUIRE TENSION SENSOR ZERO POINT" procedures See: Brakes and Traction Control/Parking Brake System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. * Before disconnecting connectors or fuses, turn the engine switch off and wait 20 seconds or more. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (ECU - ACTUATOR) (a) Disconnect the S46 ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the S32 actuator connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE ELECTRIC PARKING BRAKE ACTUATOR  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Parking Brake System/Parking Brake Actuator/Service and Repair/Removal OK --  REPLACE ELECTRIC PARKING BRAKE ECU  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Parking Brake System/Parking Brake Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal Air Inlet Damper Control Servo Motor Circuit - Air Conditioning System

dtcs.description: 42 - Air Inlet Damper Control Servo Motor Circuit The air inlet damper servo sends pulse signals to inform the air conditioning amplifier of the damper position. The air conditioning amplifier activates the motor (normal, reverse) based on the signals to move the air inlet damper to any position, which controls the intake air settings (FRESH, FRESH / RECIRCULATION and RECIRCULATION). HINT Confirm that no mechanical problem is present because this diagnostic code can be output when either a damper link or damper is mechanically locked. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (AIR INLET DAMPER TARGET PULSE) (a) Use the Data List to check if the servo motor is functioning properly. Air Conditioner OK: The display is as specified in the normal condition. Result B --  PROCEED TO NEXT CIRCUIT INSPECTION SHOWN IN PROBLEM SYMPTOMS TABLE  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Testing and Inspection/Symptom Related Diagnostic Procedures C --  REPLACE AIR CONDITIONING AMPLIFIER  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Module HVAC/Service and Repair/Removal A -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE AIR INLET SERVO MOTOR HINT Since the servo motor cannot be inspected while it is removed from the vehicle, replace the servo motor with a normal one and check that the condition returns to normal    See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Housing Assembly HVAC/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Air Conditioning Unit (for Front Side)/Disassembly. OK: Same problem does not occur. NG --  REPLACE AIR CONDITIONING HARNESS  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Housing Assembly HVAC/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Air Conditioning Unit (for Front Side)/Disassembly OK --  END Capacitor Malfunction - Electronically Controlled Brake System

dtcs.description: 43 - Capacitor Malfunction The brake control power supply (capacitor) is used as auxiliary power for brake control when battery voltage is low. PROCEDURE

(c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: OK --  RECONFIRM DTC NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR

INSPECT BATTERY (a) Check the battery voltage. Standard voltage: 11 to 14 V NG --  INSPECT CHARGING SYSTEM  See: Starting and Charging/Charging System/Testing and Inspection OK -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT BRAKE CONTROL POWER SUPPLY (+BC TERMINAL) (a) Disconnect the S49 power supply connector. (b) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR (+BC CIRCUIT) OK -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (c) Check if the same DTC is recorded    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. HINT Reinstall the sensors, connectors, etc. and restore the vehicle to its prior condition before rechecking for DTCs. dtcs.description: 43 - Actuator Malfunction NOTICE: * If the electric parking brake ECU or electric parking brake actuator is replaced, perform the "Reset Memory" and "ACQUIRE TENSION SENSOR ZERO POINT" procedures See: Brakes and Traction Control/Parking Brake System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. * Before disconnecting connectors or fuses, turn the engine switch off and wait 20 seconds or more. * This DTC may be output if a manual release cable is used. There is no malfunction. Perform the "Reset Memory" and "ACQUIRE TENSION SENSOR ZERO POINT" procedures See: Brakes and Traction Control/Parking Brake System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC (a) Check for DTC    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Parking Brake System/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Check if DTCs other than C13A7/43 are output. Result: B --  GO TO DIAGNOSTIC TROUBLE CODE CHART A -- Continue to next step.

(c) w/ Multi-display: Check that the connectors are properly connected to the center airbag sensor and the display and navigation module display. OK: The connectors are properly connected. (d) w/o Multi-display: Check that the connectors are properly connected to the center airbag sensor and the integration control and panel. OK: The connectors are properly connected. (b) w/o Multi-display: (1) Disconnect the connectors from the center airbag sensor and the integration control and panel. (f) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. (g) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. OK:

dtcs.description: 44 - Tension Sensor Malfunction The tension sensor is built into the electric parking brake actuator. It detects the tension value of the parking brake cable and outputs the value to the electric parking brake ECU. NOTICE: * If the electric parking brake ECU or electric parking brake actuator is replaced, perform the "Reset Memory" and "ACQUIRE TENSION SENSOR ZERO POINT" procedures See: Brakes and Traction Control/Parking Brake System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. * Before disconnecting connectors or fuses, turn the engine switch off and wait 20 seconds or more. * This DTC may be output if a manual release cable is used. There is no malfunction. Perform the "Reset Memory" and "ACQUIRE TENSION SENSOR ZERO POINT" procedures See: Brakes and Traction Control/Parking Brake System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 44 - Driver Side Cool Air Bypass Damper Control The damper servo (for driver side cool air bypass) sends pulse signals to inform the air conditioning amplifier of the damper position. The air conditioning amplifier activates the motor (normal, reverse) based on the signals to move the damper servo (for driver side cool air bypass) to any position. As a result, when the air conditioner is operating, the temperature of the air blowing toward the driver's head area is controlled. HINT Confirm that no mechanical problem is present because this diagnostic code can be output when either a damper link or damper is mechanically locked. PROCEDURE

OK: Same problem does not occur. NG --  REPLACE AIR CONDITIONING HARNESS  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Housing Assembly HVAC/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Air Conditioning Unit (for Front Side)/Disassembly OK --  END Transmitter ID5 Error - Tire Pressure Monitoring

As a result, when the air conditioner is operating, the temperature of the air blowing toward the passenger's head area is controlled. dtcs.description: 46 - Air Mix Damper Control Servo Motor Circuit (Driver Side) The air mix damper servo (for driver side HOT) sends pulse signals to inform the air conditioning amplifier of the damper position. The air conditioning amplifier activates the motor (normal, reverse) based on the signals to move the air mix damper (for driver side HOT) to any position. As a result the amount of air that has passed through the evaporator and is now passing through the heater core is adjusted, and the temperature of the air blowing toward the driver side is controlled. HINT Confirm that no mechanical problem is present because this diagnostic code can be output when either a damper link or damper is mechanically locked. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 46, C1281/81, C1364/61: Master Cylinder Pressure Sensor Malfunction

61 - Wheel Cylinder Pressure Sensor Malfunction The master cylinder pressure sensor and the wheel cylinder pressure sensor are built into the brake actuator. They measure the master cylinder pressure and the wheel cylinder pressure and send the data to the skid control ECU.

81 can be deleted when the master cylinder pressure sensor sends a master cylinder pressure signal or the Test Mode ends. DTC C1281/81 is output only in the Test Mode. NOTICE: When replacing the skid control ECU or brake actuator, perform initialization of linear solenoid valve and calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 49 - Open in Stop Light Switch Circuit The skid control ECU inputs the stop light switch signal and detects braking condition. The skid control ECU has a circuit for open detection. The skid control ECU outputs the DTC if it detects an open in the stop light signal input line when the stop light switch is off or an open in the stop light circuit (skid control ECU side). NOTICE: When replacing the skid control ECU, perform initialization of linear solenoid valve and calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 49 - Rear Air Outlet Damper Control Servo Motor Circuit The rear outlet damper servo (for LH side) sends pulse signals to inform the air conditioning amplifier of the damper position. The air conditioning amplifier activates the motor (normal or reverse) based on these signals to move the rear outlet damper (for LH side) to the appropriate position, which controls the air outlet modes. HINT Confirm that no mechanical problem is present because this diagnostic code can be output when either a damper link or damper is mechanically locked. NOTICE: * If this DTC is output, perform the following procedures before beginning the INSPECTION PROCEDURE. (1) Change the air outlet mode position for the rear LH and RH seats to "FOOT". (2) Turn the engine switch off, and wait approximately 90 seconds. (3) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (4) Clear DTCs See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. (5) With the air outlet mode position for the rear LH and RH seats on "FOOT", wait approximately 45 seconds. (6) Check for DTCs See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. If this DTC is not output, there is no malfunction. If this DTC is output, perform the INSPECTION PROCEDURE below. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 52 - Lost Communication with Brake System Control Module The electric parking brake ECU uses CAN communication with the ECM and skid control ECU. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and the Techstream on. (d) Clear the DTC    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Parking Brake System/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (e) Turn the engine switch off. (f) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and check for DTC. dtcs.description: 51 - Actuator Neutral Position Calibration Undone If the steering control ECU or steering actuator is replaced, or if the tie-rod ends are adjusted, VGRS system calibration is required. If the steering control ECU detects that steering angle adjustment is incomplete, it will store DTC C1591/51. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC (a) Check for DTCs    See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Variable Gear Ratio Steering System. OK:

PERFORM VGRS SYSTEM CALIBRATION (a) Perform VGRS system calibration    See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Variable Gear Ratio Steering System/Calibration. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC (a) Check for DTCs    See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Variable Gear Ratio Steering System. Result: B --  GO TO DTC C1592/52  See: C Code Charts/C1592 C --  GO TO DTC C1593/53  See: C Code Charts/C1593 A --  END Short in Driver Side Squib Circuit - Airbag System

dtcs.description: 51-B1803/51: Short in Driver Side Squib Circuit

51 - Short to B+ in Driver Side Squib Circuit * The driver side squib circuit consists of the center airbag sensor, the spiral cable, and the steering pad. * The circuit instructs the SRS to deploy when deployment conditions are met. * These DTCs are recorded when a malfunction is detected in the driver side squib circuit. NOTICE: After the engine switch is turned off, the HDD navigation system requires approximately 6 minutes to record various types of memory and settings. As a result, after turning the engine switch off, wait 6 minutes or more before disconnecting the cable from the negative (-) battery terminal. HINT * Perform the simulation method by selecting "check mode" (signal check) with the Techstream See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Check Mode Procedure. * After selecting "check mode" (signal check), perform the simulation method by wiggling each connector of the airbag system or driving the vehicle on various types of roads See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK STEERING PAD (DRIVER SIDE SQUIB) (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Disconnect the connectors from the steering pad. (d) Connect the white wire side of SST (resistance: 2.1 Ohms) to connector E (orange connector).

CHECK CONNECTOR (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Disconnect SST from connector E. (d) Check that the spiral cable connectors (on the steering pad side) are not damaged. OK: The lock button is not disengaged, or the claw of the lock is not deformed or damaged. NG --  REPLACE SPIRAL CABLE  See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Clockspring Assembly / Spiral Cable/Service and Repair/Components OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DRIVER SIDE SQUIB CIRCUIT (a) Disconnect the connectors from the center airbag sensor. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (d) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: (e) Turn the engine switch off. (f) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (g) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: (h) Release the activation prevention mechanism built into connector B    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System. (i) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  CHECK INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE (CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR - SPIRAL CABLE) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR (a) Connect the connectors to the steering pad and the center airbag sensor. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. (d) Clear the DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (e) Turn the engine switch off. (f) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. (g) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. OK: DTC B1800, B1801, B1802, B1803 or 51 is not output. HINT Codes other than DTC B1800, B1801, B1802, B1803 and 51 may be output at this time, but they are not related to this check. NG --  REPLACE CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Control Module/Service and Repair/Components OK --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System

CHECK INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE (CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR - SPIRAL CABLE) (a) Restore the released activation prevention mechanism of connector B to the original condition. (b) Disconnect the instrument panel wire connector from the spiral cable. (c) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (d) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (e) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: (f) Turn the engine switch off. (g) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (h) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: (i) Release the activation prevention mechanism built into connector B    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System. (j) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE OK --  REPLACE SPIRAL CABLE  See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Clockspring Assembly / Spiral Cable/Service and Repair/Components Compressor Solenoid Circuit - Air Conditioning System

dtcs.description: 51 - Compressor Solenoid Circuit In this circuit, the compressor receives a refrigerant compression demand signal from the air conditioning amplifier. Based on this signal, the compressor changes the degree of refrigerant compression. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (REFRIGERANT CONTROL CURRENT) (a) Use the Data List to check if the refrigerant control current is functioning properly. Air Conditioner OK: The display is as specified in the normal condition. Result B --  PROCEED TO NEXT CIRCUIT INSPECTION SHOWN IN PROBLEM SYMPTOMS TABLE  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Testing and Inspection/Symptom Related Diagnostic Procedures C --  REPLACE AIR CONDITIONING AMPLIFIER  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Module HVAC/Service and Repair/Removal A -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT COMPRESSOR AND PULLEY (a) Disconnect the compressor and pulley connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below.
