CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (COMPRESSOR AND PULLEY - AIR CONDITIONING AMPLIFIER) (a) Disconnect the E11 compressor and pulley connector. (b) Disconnect the L76 amplifier connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (COMPRESSOR AND PULLEY - BODY GROUND) (a) Disconnect the E11 compressor and pulley connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE AIR CONDITIONING AMPLIFIER  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Module HVAC/Service and Repair/Removal Control Module Communication Bus OFF - Electric Parking Brake System

dtcs.description: 52 - Actuator Neutral Position Calibration Incomplete The steering control ECU first checks the actuator angle neutral position calibration flag after entering test mode. If the ECU detects that steering actuator angle neutral position calibration is incomplete twice, it will store DTC C1592/52. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CLEAR DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Variable Gear Ratio Steering System. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM VGRS SYSTEM CALIBRATION (a) Perform VGRS system calibration    See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Variable Gear Ratio Steering System/Calibration. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC (a) Check for DTCs    See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Variable Gear Ratio Steering System. Result: B --  REPLACE STEERING CONTROL ECU  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Steering and Suspension/Relays and Modules - Steering/Steering Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal C --  CLEAR DTC D --  REPLACE STEERING ACTUATOR (POWER STEERING LINK ASSEMBLY)  See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Steering Gear/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Steering Gear (w/ VGRS)/Removal A --  END Brake Booster Pump Motor on Time Abnormally Long - Electronically Controlled Brake System

dtcs.description: 52, C1253/53: Brake Booster Pump Motor on Time Abnormally Long

53 - Pump Motor Relay Malfunction The skid control ECU detects decreases in the accumulator pressure according to the data from the accumulator pressure sensor, and then starts and stops the pump motor by operating the motor relay. The skid control ECU usually drives the ABS motor relay (ABS MTR) for electronically controlled brake system control, and the ABS motor relay 2 (ABS MTR2) for ABS control. If either is of them malfunctioning, the other will substitute. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: INSPECT FUSE (ABS MTR1, ABS MTR2, ABS MAIN1 AND ABS MAIN2) (a) Remove the ABS MTR1, ABS MTR2, ABS MAIN1 and ABS MAIN2 fuses from the engine room No. 2 relay block. (b) Measure the resistance of the fuses. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE FUSE OK -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM ACTIVE TEST USING TECHSTREAM (ABS RELAY) (a) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (b) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (c) Select the Active Test mode on the Techstream. HINT * The ABS relay is a semiconductor relay. It does not emit operation sounds. * The ABS relay cannot be removed and inspected, as it is part of the engine room No. 2 relay block. (d) Remove the terminal cover. ABS/VSC/TRAC (e) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: NG --  CHECK TERMINAL VOLTAGE (ENGINE ROOM NO. 2 JUNCTION BLOCK) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK TERMINAL VOLTAGE (BM1) (a) Disconnect the A23 pump connector. (b) Read value of the Techstream (ABS MTR relay). (1) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (2) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (3) Select the Active Test mode on the Techstream. ABS/VSC/TRAC (c) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM ACTIVE TEST USING TECHSTREAM (ABS MTR2 RELAY) (a) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (b) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (c) Select the Active Test mode on the Techstream. ABS/VSC/TRAC (d) Check the operation sound of the ABS MTR2 relay and motor when operating it with the Techstream. OK: The operation sound of the ABS MTR 2 relay and motor are heard. NG --  INSPECT ABS MOTOR RELAY 2 (ABS MTR2) OK -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT BRAKE ACCUMULATOR PUMP (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Disconnect the A23 and A24 pump connectors. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE BRAKE ACCUMULATOR PUMP  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Hydraulic Control Assembly - Antilock Brakes/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (SKID CONTROL ECU - BRAKE ACCUMULATOR PUMP) (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Disconnect the A19 ECU connector. (c) Disconnect the A23 and A24 pump connectors. (d) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (ACCUMULATOR SENSOR) (a) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (b) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (c) Select the Data List mode on the Techstream. ABS/VSC/TRAC (d) Depress the brake pedal 4 or 5 times to operate the pump motor, and check the output value on the Techstream with the motor stopped (not braking) for 30 seconds. OK: The accumulator pressure sensor voltage change is within 0.2 V. HINT * This inspection is performed to determine if there is a long term power malfunction when low pressure is detected in the accumulator due to a sensor malfunction, fluid leak in the actuator, or accumulator deterioration. * If the reservoir fluid level drops, there may be a fluid leak. NG --  PERFORM ACTIVE TEST USING TECHSTREAM (SMC1, SMC2, SLRRR, AND SLRRL) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK TERMINAL VOLTAGE (ENGINE ROOM NO. 2 JUNCTION BLOCK) (a) Disconnect the 2F junction block connector. (b) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT ENGINE ROOM NO. 2 RELAY BLOCK AND NO. 2 JUNCTION BLOCK (INNER CIRCUIT) (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Disconnect the 2B and 2F junction block connectors. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE ENGINE ROOM NO. 2 RELAY BLOCK AND NO. 2 JUNCTION BLOCK OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (SKID CONTROL ECU - ENGINE ROOM NO. 2 JUNCTION BLOCK) (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Disconnect the A19 ECU connector. (c) Disconnect the 2G junction block connector. (d) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE ENGINE ROOM NO. 2 JUNCTION BLOCK

INSPECT ABS MOTOR RELAY 2 (ABS MTR2) (a) Remove the ABS motor relay 2 (ABS MTR2). (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE ABS MOTOR RELAY 2 (ABS MTR2)  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Brake Fluid Pump Relay/Testing and Inspection OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK TERMINAL VOLTAGE (NO. 3 RELAY BLOCK) (a) Remove the ABS motor relay 2 (ABS MTR2). (b) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (BRAKE ACCUMULATOR PUMP - NO. 3 RELAY BLOCK) (a) Disconnect the A24 pump connector. (b) Remove the ABS motor relay 2 (ABS MTR2). (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (SKID CONTROL ECU - NO. 3 RELAY BLOCK) (a) Disconnect the A21 ECU connector. (b) Remove the ABS motor relay 2 (ABS MTR2). (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (c) Check if the same DTCs are recorded    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. HINT Reinstall the sensors, connectors, etc. and restore the vehicle to its prior condition before rechecking for DTCs. Result B --  REPLACE SKID CONTROL ECU  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Electronic Brake Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal A --  END

PERFORM ACTIVE TEST USING TECHSTREAM (SMC1, SMC2, SLRRR, AND SLRRL) (a) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (b) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (c) Turn the Techstream main switch on. (d) Select the Active Test mode on the Techstream. HINT The Active Test can be performed when the following conditions are met. * Main relay 1 and 2 are on. * Shift lever is P position. * Parking brake is on. * Vehicle speed is 0 km/h (0 mph). ABS/VSC/TRAC (e) Using the Techstream, turn SMC1, SMC2, SLRRR, or SLRRL ON. (f) Check that each wheel cylinder's output value does not increase. OK: Each wheel cylinder's output value does not increase. HINT If any of wheel cylinder's output value increases, there may be a brake fluid leak in the brake actuator. NG --  REPLACE BRAKE ACTUATOR  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Hydraulic Control Assembly - Antilock Brakes/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal OK --  REPLACE BRAKE ACCUMULATOR PUMP  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Hydraulic Control Assembly - Antilock Brakes/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal Short in Front Passenger Side Squib Circuit - Airbag System

dtcs.description: 52-B1808/52: Short in Front Passenger Side Squib Circuit

52 - Short to B+ in Front Passenger Side Squib Circuit The front passenger side squib circuit consists of the center airbag sensor and the front passenger airbag. The circuit instructs the SRS to deploy when deployment conditions are met. These DTCs are recorded when a malfunction is detected in the front passenger side squib circuit. NOTICE: After the engine switch is turned off, the HDD navigation system requires approximately 6 minutes to record various types of memory and settings. As a result, after turning the engine switch off, wait 6 minutes or more before disconnecting the cable from the negative (-) battery terminal. HINT * Perform the simulation method by selecting "check mode" (signal check) with the Techstream See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Check Mode Procedure. * After selecting "check mode" (signal check), perform the simulation method by wiggling each connector of the airbag system or driving the vehicle on various types of roads See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System. PROCEDURE

(b) Disconnect the instrument panel wire connector from the instrument panel wire assembly. (c) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. dtcs.description: 53 - Driver Side Air Outlet Damper COOL Control Servo Motor Circuit The air mix damper servo (for driver side COOL) sends pulse signals to inform the air conditioning amplifier of the damper position. The air conditioning amplifier activates the motor (normal, reverse) based on the signals to move the air mix damper (for driver side COOL) to any position. As a result the amount of air that has passed through the evaporator and is now passing through the heater core is adjusted, and the temperature of the air blowing toward the driver side is controlled. HINT Confirm that no mechanical problem is present because this diagnostic code can be output when either a damper link or damper is mechanically locked. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (AIR MIX SERVO MOTOR TARGET PULSE (for Driver Side COOL)) (a) Use the Data List to check if the servo motor is functioning properly. Air Conditioner OK: The display is as specified in the normal condition. Result B --  PROCEED TO NEXT CIRCUIT INSPECTION SHOWN IN PROBLEM SYMPTOMS TABLE  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Testing and Inspection/Symptom Related Diagnostic Procedures C --  REPLACE AIR CONDITIONING AMPLIFIER  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Module HVAC/Service and Repair/Removal A -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE AIR MIX SERVO MOTOR (for Driver Side COOL) HINT Since the servo motor cannot be inspected while it is removed from the vehicle, replace the servo motor with a normal one and check that the condition returns to normal    See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Housing Assembly HVAC/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Air Conditioning Unit (for Front Side)/Disassembly. OK: Same problem does not occur. NG --  REPLACE AIR CONDITIONING HARNESS  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Housing Assembly HVAC/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Air Conditioning Unit (for Front Side)/Disassembly OK --  END Short in Driver Side Squib 2nd Step Circuit - Airbag System

(d) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (e) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: (f) Turn the engine switch off. dtcs.description: 54 - Accumulator Pressure Sensor Malfunction The accumulator pressure sensor is built into the brake actuator. The skid control ECU detects the accumulator pressure based on the data sent from the accumulator pressure sensor, and then runs and stops the pump motor by operating the motor relay. NOTICE: When replacing the skid control ECU or brake actuator, perform initialization of linear solenoid valve and calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (SKID CONTROL ECU - BRAKE ACTUATOR) (a) Disconnect the A19 ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the A27 actuator connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: (d) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (c) Check if the same DTC is recorded    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. HINT Reinstall the sensors, connectors, etc. and restore the vehicle to its prior condition before rechecking for DTCs. Result B --  END A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FREEZE FRAME DATA (a) Check the information code from the "FREEZE FRAME DATA" memorized when the DTC (C1365/54) is stored    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Freeze Frame Data. Result B --  REPLACE BRAKE ACTUATOR  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Hydraulic Control Assembly - Antilock Brakes/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal A --  REPLACE SKID CONTROL ECU  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Electronic Brake Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal Passenger Side Air Outlet Damper COOL Control Servo Motor Circuit - Air Conditioning System

dtcs.description: 54 - Passenger Side Air Outlet Damper COOL Control Servo Motor Circuit The air mix damper servo (for passenger side COOL) sends pulse signals to inform the air conditioning amplifier of the damper position. The air conditioning amplifier activates the motor (normal, reverse) based on the signals to move the air mix damper (for passenger side COOL) to any position. As a result the amount of air that has passed through the evaporator and is now passing through the heater core is adjusted, and the temperature of the air blowing toward the passenger side is controlled. HINT Confirm that no mechanical problem is present because this diagnostic code can be output when either a damper link or damper is mechanically locked. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (AIR MIX SERVO MOTOR TARGET PULSE (for Passenger Side COOL)) (a) Use the Data List to check if the servo motor is functioning properly. Air Conditioner OK: The display is as specified in the normal condition. Result B --  PROCEED TO NEXT CIRCUIT INSPECTION SHOWN IN PROBLEM SYMPTOMS TABLE  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Testing and Inspection/Symptom Related Diagnostic Procedures C --  REPLACE AIR CONDITIONING AMPLIFIER  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Module HVAC/Service and Repair/Removal A -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE AIR MIX SERVO MOTOR (for Passenger Side COOL) HINT Since the servo motor cannot be inspected while it is removed from the vehicle, replace the servo motor with a normal one and check that the condition returns to normal    See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Housing Assembly HVAC/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Air Conditioning Unit (for Front Side)/Disassembly. OK: Same problem does not occur. NG --  REPLACE AIR CONDITIONING HARNESS  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Housing Assembly HVAC/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Air Conditioning Unit (for Front Side)/Disassembly OK --  END Short in Front Passenger Side Squib 2nd Step Circuit - Airbag System

dtcs.description: 54-B1818/54: Short in Front Passenger Side Squib 2nd Step Circuit

54 - Short to B+ in Front Passenger Side Squib 2nd Step Circuit The front passenger side squib 2nd step circuit consists of the center airbag sensor and the front passenger airbag. The circuit instructs the SRS to deploy when deployment conditions are met. These DTCs are recorded when a malfunction is detected in the front passenger side squib 2nd step circuit. NOTICE: After the engine switch is turned off, the HDD navigation system requires approximately 6 minutes to record various types of memory and settings. As a result, after turning the engine switch off, wait 6 minutes or more before disconnecting the cable from the negative (-) battery terminal. HINT * Perform the simulation method by selecting "check mode" (signal check) with the Techstream See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Check Mode Procedure. * After selecting "check mode" (signal check), perform the simulation method by wiggling each connector of the airbag system or driving the vehicle on various types of roads See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK FRONT PASSENGER AIRBAG (FRONT PASSENGER SIDE SQUIB 2ND STEP) (a) Turn the engine switch off. (d) Connect the white wire side of SST (resistance: 2.1 Ohms) to connector E (black connector).

CHECK CONNECTOR (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Disconnect SST from connector E. (d) Check that the instrument panel wire connectors (on the front passenger airbag side) are not damaged. OK: The lock button is not disengaged, or the claw of the lock is not damaged or deformed. NG --  REPLACE INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE ASSEMBLY OK -- Continue to next step.

dtcs.description: 55, C15C4/68: Lost Communication with Steering Angle Sensor Module

68 - Steering Angle Signal (Test Mode DTC) Signal transmission between the steering control ECU and steering angle sensor is performed via serial communication. If an error occurs between them in the serial communication, the ECU will store DTC C1595/55. HINT *: A tire angle of 36° and a motor rotation angle of 36° correspond to the steering wheel angle. The amount of change in tire angle and motor rotation angle is from when the mode is changed to test mode. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC (CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM) (a) Check for DTCs in the CAN communication system    See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/CAN Communication System/Diagnosis System. Result B --  GO TO CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM (HOW TO PROCEED WITH TROUBLESHOOTING)  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/CAN Communication System/How to Proceed With Troubleshooting A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK STEERING ANGLE SENSOR (a) Check if a steering angle sensor that complies with the vehicle specifications is installed. OK: A steering angle sensor that complies with the vehicle specifications is installed. NG --  REPLACE STEERING ANGLE SENSOR  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Steering Angle Sensor/Service and Repair/Removal OK -- Continue to next step.

Standard resistance: (h) Release the activation prevention mechanism built into connector B    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System. (i) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE FLOOR WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR (a) Connect the connectors to the curtain shield airbag RH and the center airbag sensor. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. (d) Clear the DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (e) Turn the engine switch off. (f) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. dtcs.description: 58 - Passenger Side Air Outlet Damper Control Servo Motor Circuit The air mix damper servo (for passenger side front A/C rear air flow) sends pulse signals to inform the air conditioning amplifier of the damper position. The air conditioning amplifier activates the motor (normal, reverse) based on the signals to move the air mix damper (for passenger side front A/C rear air flow) to any position. As a result the amount of air that has passed through the evaporator and is now passing through the heater core is adjusted, and the temperature of the air blowing toward the rear passenger side is controlled. HINT Confirm that no mechanical problem is present because this diagnostic code can be output when either a damper link or damper is mechanically locked. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (AIR MIX SERVO MOTOR (for P Side Front A/C Rear Air Flow)) (a) Use the Data List to check if the servo motor is functioning properly. Air Conditioner OK: The display is as specified in the normal condition. Result B --  PROCEED TO NEXT CIRCUIT INSPECTION SHOWN IN PROBLEM SYMPTOMS TABLE  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Testing and Inspection/Symptom Related Diagnostic Procedures C --  REPLACE AIR CONDITIONING AMPLIFIER  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Module HVAC/Service and Repair/Removal A -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE AIR MIX SERVO MOTOR (for Passenger Side Front A/C Rear Air Flow) HINT Since the servo motor cannot be inspected while it is removed from the vehicle, replace the servo motor with a normal one and check that the condition returns to normal    See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Housing Assembly HVAC/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Air Conditioning Unit (for Front Side)/Disassembly. OK: Same problem does not occur. NG --  REPLACE AIR CONDITIONING HARNESS  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Housing Assembly HVAC/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Air Conditioning Unit (for Front Side)/Disassembly OK --  END Short in Curtain Shield Squib LH Circuit - Airbag System

dtcs.description: 58-B1838/58: Short in Curtain Shield Squib LH Circuit

58 - Short to B+ in Curtain Shield Squib LH Circuit The curtain shield squib LH circuit consists of the center airbag sensor and the curtain shield airbag LH. The circuit instructs the SRS to deploy when deployment conditions are met. These DTCs are recorded when a malfunction is detected in the curtain shield squib LH circuit. NOTICE: After the engine switch is turned off, the HDD navigation system requires approximately 6 minutes to record various types of memory and settings. As a result, after turning the engine switch off, wait 6 minutes or more before disconnecting the cable from the negative (-) battery terminal. HINT * Perform the simulation method by selecting "check mode" (signal check) with the Techstream See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Check Mode Procedure. * After selecting "check mode" (signal check), perform the simulation method by wiggling each connector of the airbag system or driving the vehicle on various types of roads See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK CURTAIN SHIELD AIRBAG LH (CURTAIN SHIELD SQUIB) (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Disconnect the connectors from the curtain shield airbag LH. (d) Connect the white wire side of SST (resistance: 2.1 Ohms) to connector C.

CHECK CONNECTOR (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Disconnect SST from connector C. (d) Check that the No. 2 floor wire connectors (on the curtain shield airbag LH side) are not damaged. OK: The lock button is not disengaged, or the claw of the lock is not deformed or damaged. NG --  REPLACE NO. 2 FLOOR WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK NO. 2 FLOOR WIRE (a) Disconnect the connectors from the center airbag sensor. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (f) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (g) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: (h) Release the activation prevention mechanism built into connector B    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System. (i) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE NO. 2 FLOOR WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR (a) Connect the connectors to the curtain shield airbag LH and the center airbag sensor. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. (d) Clear the DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (e) Turn the engine switch off. (f) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. (g) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. OK: DTC B1835, B1836, B1837, B1838 or 58 is not output. HINT Codes other than DTC B1835, B1836, B1837, B1838 and 58 may be output at this time, but they are not related to this check. dtcs.description: 59 - Max Hot Bypass Damper HOT Control Circuit The max hot bypass damper servo motor (for HOT) sends pulse signals to inform the air conditioning amplifier of the damper position. The air conditioning amplifier activates the motor (normal, reverse) based on the signals to move the max hot bypass damper servo motor (for HOT) to any position. As a result, when the air conditioner is operating, the temperature of the air blowing toward the driver's head area is controlled. HINT Confirm that no mechanical problem is present because this diagnostic code can be output when either a damper link or damper is mechanically locked. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (MAX HOT BYPASS DAMPER SERVO MOTOR TARGET PULSE (for HOT)) (a) Use the Data List to check if the servo motor is functioning properly. Air Conditioner OK: The display is as specified in the normal condition. OK: Same problem does not occur. NG --  REPLACE AIR CONDITIONING HARNESS  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Housing Assembly HVAC/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Air Conditioning Unit (for Front Side)/Disassembly OK --  END DTC 60-10 AUDIO / VISUAL: AUDIO AND VISUAL SYSTEM (w/o Multi-display): 60-10,60-11: AM Tuner PLL does not Lock 60-11 - FM Tuner PLL does not Lock HINT After the inspection is completed, clear the DTCs. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 61 - Brake Control System Malfunction The electric parking brake ECU receives vehicle wheel speed information from the skid control ECU via CAN communication. NOTICE: If the electric parking brake ECU or electric parking brake actuator is replaced, perform the "Reset Memory" and "ACQUIRE TENSION SENSOR ZERO POINT" procedures See: Brakes and Traction Control/Parking Brake System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. PROCEDURE

(d) Check for DTC    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (d) Clear the DTC    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Parking Brake System/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (e) Check for DTC. Result: B --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting A --  REPLACE ELECTRIC PARKING BRAKE ECU  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Parking Brake System/Parking Brake Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal Actuator Malfunction - Variable Gear Ratio Steering System

dtcs.description: 61 - Actuator Malfunction The steering control ECU drives the actuator while the steering wheel is turned to set the gear ratio. It detects the current flowing into the actuator motor driving circuit. If the steering control ECU detects excessive current, it will store this DTC C15A1/61, turn on the master warning light, and stop VGRS operation. HINT When DTC C15A2/62 is output, a short circuit inside the steering actuator or inside the steering control ECU is suspected. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (STEERING CONTROL ECU - STEERING ACTUATOR) (a) Disconnect the A43 steering control ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the x1 steering actuator connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK STEERING ACTUATOR (a) Disconnect the A43 steering control ECU connector. HINT The resistance must be measured after the steering actuator connector is connected. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE STEERING ACTUATOR (POWER STEERING LINK ASSEMBLY)  See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Steering Gear/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Steering Gear (w/ VGRS)/Removal OK --  REPLACE STEERING CONTROL ECU  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Steering and Suspension/Relays and Modules - Steering/Steering Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal Emission Gas NOx Sensor Circuit - Air Conditioning System

dtcs.description: 61 - Emission Gas NOx Sensor Circuit The exhaust gas sensor, which is installed on the front of the condenser, automatically changes to FRESH, FRESH / RECIRCULATION, or RECIRCULATION. The exhaust gas sensor detects NOx in the emission gas and transmits signals to the front light ECU (front controller). The front light ECU (front controller) sends the read signal to the air conditioning amplifier via CAN communication line. HINT This circuit uses the CAN communication line. Before performing the inspection, check that the CAN communication system is functioning normally. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: INSPECT FUSE (E/G RM-IG1-2) (a) Remove the E/G RM-IG1-2 fuse from the engine room No. 2 relay block. (b) Measure the resistance of the fuse. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE FUSE OK -- Continue to next step.

Air Conditioner OK: The display is as specified in the normal condition. dtcs.description: 62 - Deceleration Sensor The electric parking brake ECU receives vehicle tilt information from the yaw rate and acceleration sensor via CAN communication. HINT *: The G sensor is judged to be stuck when the G sensor is stuck and the vehicle is driven and stopped 16 times. NOTICE: If the electric parking brake ECU or electric parking brake actuator is replaced, perform the "Reset Memory" and "ACQUIRE TENSION SENSOR ZERO POINT" procedures See: Brakes and Traction Control/Parking Brake System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 62, C15A6/62: Actuator Malfunction

62 - Actuator Malfunction The steering control ECU drives the actuator based on steering angle sensor signals and vehicle speed signals. If the steering control ECU detects excessive current flowing into the steering actuator, or an internal malfunction, it will turn on the master warning light, store DTC C15A2/62, and stop VGRS operation. If the steering control ECU detects a malfunction in the pre driver circuit, it will turn the master warning light on, store DTC C15A6/62, and stop VGRS operation. HINT The actuator only can be checked in this procedure. If it is normal, the steering control ECU is faulty. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 62-C1344/65: Front Hydraulic System RH Malfunction

65 - Rear Hydraulic System LH Malfunction The skid control ECU inputs the operating fluid pressure of each wheel cylinder using the wheel cylinder pressure sensors in order to control the braking force of each wheel. DTCs may be stored if brake fluid leaks, a wheel cylinder vibrates due to uneven wear of the brake disc rotor, or foreign matter enters the solenoid valve. DTCs may be stored if the line pressure drops during air bleeding or brake pad is replaced. NOTICE: When replacing the brake actuator, perform initialization of linear solenoid valve and calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. HINT When C1364/61 is output together with C1341/62, C1342/63, C1343/64 and C1344/65, inspect and repair the trouble areas indicated by C1364/61 first. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK BRAKE FLUID LEAKAGE (a) Check that there is no fluid leakage in the brake line between the brake actuator and the wheel cylinder which is the cause of DTCs. (b) Check for a brake fluid leak from the connection between the accumulator pump and brake actuator    See: C Code Charts/C1202. (c) Check that the brake is not dragging. OK: There is no fluid leakage or dragging. NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE APPLICABLE PART OK -- Continue to next step.
PERFORM AIR BLEED (a) Bleed the air from the front and rear brake systems    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Brake Bleeding/Service and Repair. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

(c) Check if the same DTCs are recorded    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. dtcs.description: 62-B1853/62: Short in Rear Side Squib RH Circuit

62 - Short to B+ in Rear Side Squib RH Circuit The rear side squib RH circuit consists of the center airbag sensor and the rear seat side airbag RH. This circuit instructs the SRS to deploy when deployment conditions are met. These DTCs are recorded when a malfunction is detected in the rear side squib RH circuit. NOTICE: After the engine switch is turned off, the HDD navigation system requires approximately 6 minutes to record various types of memory and settings. dtcs.description: 63, C15C5/69: Actuator Malfunction

69 - Motor Revolution Angle Signal (Test Mode DTC) The steering actuator drives the internal motor using the current output from the steering control ECU to change the relative angle between the tire angle and steering wheel angle. The rotation angle sensor in the steering actuator detects the motor rotation angle and outputs this information to the steering control ECU. If the steering control ECU detects a malfunction in the rotation angle sensor circuit, it will store DTC C15A3/63. HINT *: A tire angle of 36° and a motor rotation angle of 36° correspond to the steering wheel angle. The amount of change in tire angle and motor rotation angle is from when the mode is changed to test mode. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (STEERING CONTROL ECU - STEERING ACTUATOR) (a) Disconnect the A41 steering control ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the x2 steering actuator connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK STEERING ACTUATOR (a) Disconnect the A41 steering control ECU connector. HINT The resistance must be measured after the steering actuator connector is connected. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE STEERING ACTUATOR (POWER STEERING LINK ASSEMBLY)  See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Steering Gear/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Steering Gear (w/ VGRS)/Removal OK --  REPLACE STEERING CONTROL ECU  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Steering and Suspension/Relays and Modules - Steering/Steering Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal Control Module Communication Bus OFF- Electronically Controlled Brake System

dtcs.description: 63-B1858/63: Short in Rear Side Squib LH Circuit

63 - Short to B+ in Rear Side Squib LH Circuit The rear side squib LH circuit consists of the center airbag sensor and the rear seat side airbag LH. The circuit instructs the SRS to deploy when deployment conditions are met. These DTCs are recorded when a malfunction is detected in the rear side squib LH circuit. NOTICE: After the engine switch is turned off, the HDD navigation system requires approximately 6 minutes to record various types of memory and settings. As a result, after turning the engine switch off, wait 6 minutes or more before disconnecting the cable from the negative (-) battery terminal. HINT * Perform the simulation method by selecting "check mode" (signal check) with the Techstream See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Check Mode Procedure. * After selecting "check mode" (signal check), perform the simulation method by wiggling each connector of the airbag system or driving the vehicle on various types of roads See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK REAR SEAT SIDE AIRBAG LH (REAR SIDE SQUIB LH) (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Disconnect the connector from the rear seat side airbag LH. (d) Connect the red wire side of SST (resistance: 2.1 Ohms) to connector E.

CHECK NO. 2 FLOOR WIRE (REAR SIDE SQUIB LH CIRCUIT) (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Disconnect SST (resistance: 2.1 Ohms) from the No. 2 floor wire. (d) Disconnect the connectors from the center airbag sensor. (e) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (f) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (g) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: (h) Turn the engine switch off. (i) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (j) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: (k) Release the activation prevention mechanism built into connector B    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System. (l) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: OK --  CHECK CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR NG -- Continue to next step.

CHECK REAR SEAT WIRE LH (a) Disconnect the No. 2 floor wire connector from the rear seat wire LH. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (d) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: (e) Turn the engine switch off. (f) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (g) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: (h) Release the activation prevention mechanism built into connector D    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System. (i) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE REAR SEAT WIRE LH OK --  REPLACE NO. 2 FLOOR WIRE

CHECK CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR (a) Connect the connectors to the rear seat side airbag LH and the center airbag sensor. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. (d) Clear the DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (e) Turn the engine switch off. (f) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. (g) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. OK: DTC B1855, B1856, B1857, B1858 or 63 is not output. HINT Codes other than DTC B1855, B1856, B1857, B1858 and 63 may be output at this time, but they are not related to this check. NG --  REPLACE CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Control Module/Service and Repair/Components OK --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System DTC 63-7D AUDIO / VISUAL: AUDIO AND VISUAL SYSTEM (w/o Multi-display): 62-7D-62-7F,63-7D-63-7F: Disc Playback Problem 63-7F - Copyright Protection Error HINT After the inspection is completed, clear the DTCs. dtcs.description: 64, C15A9/66, C15AA/66, C15AB/66: Actuator Malfunction

66 - Lock Mechanism Insertion Malfunction When the VGRS system is normal, the steering actuator conducts current from the steering control ECU to the steering actuator solenoid to release the lock mechanism, enabling motor operation. The steering actuator does not operate under either of the following conditions: * The engine is off. * The motor in the actuator is locked by the lock mechanism to prevent rotation (fail-safe function). * The system is being protected from overheating (fail-safe function). If the steering control ECU detects a malfunction in the steering actuator lock mechanism or a motor rotation angle error, it will store these DTCs. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: REPLACE STEERING ACTUATOR (POWER STEERING LINK ASSEMBLY) (a) Replace steering actuator (power steering gear assembly)    See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Steering Gear/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Steering Gear (w/ VGRS)/Removal. NOTICE: Before replacing the steering actuator (power steering gear assembly), make sure to use Data List to confirm and record the "Elec Angle Position" value    See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Variable Gear Ratio Steering System/Data List / Active Test. NEXT --  END Front Hydraulic System RH Malfunction - Electronically Controlled Brake System

dtcs.description: 64-B1863/64: Short in Driver Side Knee Airbag Squib Circuit

64 - Short to B+ in Driver Side Knee Airbag Squib Circuit The driver side knee airbag squib circuit consists of the center airbag sensor and the driver side knee airbag. This circuit instructs the SRS to deploy when deployment conditions are met. These DTCs are recorded when a malfunction is detected in the driver side knee airbag squib circuit. NOTICE: After the engine switch is turned off, the HDD navigation system requires approximately 6 minutes to record various types of memory and settings. As a result, after turning the engine switch off, wait 6 minutes or more before disconnecting the cable from the negative (-) battery terminal. HINT * Perform the simulation method by selecting "check mode" (signal check) with the Techstream See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Check Mode Procedure. * After selecting "check mode" (signal check), perform the simulation method by wiggling each connector of the airbag system or driving the vehicle on various types of roads See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DRIVER SIDE KNEE AIRBAG (DRIVER SIDE KNEE AIRBAG SQUIB) (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Disconnect the connector from the driver side knee airbag. (d) Connect the white wire side of SST (resistance: 2.1 Ohms) to connector C.

CHECK CONNECTOR (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Disconnect SST from connector C. (d) Check that the instrument panel wire connector (on the driver side knee airbag side) is not damaged. OK: The lock button is not disengaged, or the claw of the lock is not deformed or damaged. NG --  REPLACE INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE (DRIVER SIDE KNEE AIRBAG CIRCUIT) (a) Disconnect the connectors from the center airbag sensor. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (d) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: (e) Turn the engine switch off. (f) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (g) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: (h) Release the activation prevention mechanism built into connector B    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System. (i) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR (a) Connect the connectors to the driver side knee airbag and the center airbag sensor. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. (d) Clear the DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (e) Turn the engine switch off. (f) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. (g) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. OK: DTC B1860, B1861, B1862, B1863 or 64 is not output. HINT Codes other than DTC B1860, B1861, B1862, B1863 and 64 may be output at this time, but they are not related to this check. NG --  REPLACE CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Control Module/Service and Repair/Components OK --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System Lost Communication with Engine ECU - Electronically Controlled Brake System

65 - Short to B+ in Front Passenger Side Knee Airbag Squib Circuit The front passenger side knee airbag squib circuit consists of the center airbag sensor and the front passenger side knee airbag. The circuit instructs the SRS to deploy when deployment conditions are met. These DTCs are recorded when a malfunction is detected in the front passenger side knee airbag squib circuit. NOTICE: After the engine switch is turned off, the HDD navigation system requires approximately 6 minutes to record various types of memory and settings. As a result, after turning the engine switch off, wait 6 minutes or more before disconnecting the cable from the negative (-) battery terminal.
