dtcs.description: 65-C2169/69: Abnormal Temperature Inside ID1 Tire

69 - Abnormal Temperature Inside ID5 Tire Each tire pressure warning valve and transmitter measures the internal temperature of its tire as well as tire pressure, and transmits the information to the tire pressure warning ECU along with the transmitter ID. If the measured temperature is out of the specified range, the tire pressure warning ECU recognizes it as a malfunction and outputs DTCs, and the tire pressure warning light blinks for 1 minute and then remains on. HINT It is necessary to perform the procedure to identify the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter that is malfunctioning because it cannot be identified by the output DTC. NOTICE: * When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and note them down before removal. * It is necessary to perform initialization See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization after registration See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter has been replaced. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK TIRES (a) Check that the tires are not flat, and have no indication of tire pressure drop. OK: Tires are normal. HINT If a tire is damaged, the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter may also have been damaged at the same time. NG --  REPLACE TIRE AND TIRE PRESSURE WARNING VALVE AND TRANSMITTER  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and
IDENTIFY TRANSMITTER CORRESPONDING TO DTC   See: C Code Charts/C2141 NEXT -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING VALVE AND TRANSMITTER (a) Replace the identified tire pressure warning valve and transmitter with a new one    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Sensor/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal. HINT * Before installing a new tire pressure warning valve and transmitter, read and write down its transmitter ID. * The IDs for the tire pressure warning valve and transmitters that are not replaced should be checked using the Techstream and recorded. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM REGISTRATION OF TRANSMITTER ID (a) Register the transmitter IDs for all wheels    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM INITIALIZATION (a) Perform initialization    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CONFIRM TIRE INFLATION PRESSURE (DATA LIST)   See: C Code Charts/C2121 B --  END A --  REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Module/Service and Repair/Removal Abnormal Temperature Inside ID2 Tire - Tire Pressure Monitoring

dtcs.description: 66, C1368/67: Linear Solenoid Valve Offset Learning Undone

67 - Linear Solenoid Valve Offset Malfunction The skid control ECU stores and corrects the difference in each individual part such as the stroke sensor, actuator solenoids. Perform initialization of linear solenoid valve and calibration if these parts are replaced. The DTCs are canceled when the result of linear valve offset learning is normal. NOTICE: When replacing the brake actuator, perform initialization of linear solenoid valve and calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Turn the engine switch off. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (d) Check that the DTCs (electronically controlled brake system and SFI system) are recorded. Result B --  GO TO SFI SYSTEM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How to Proceed with Troubleshooting C --  CHECK FOR CIRCUIT INDICATED BY OUTPUT CODE  See: Diagnostic Trouble Code Descriptions/Electronically Controlled Brake System A -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM INITIALIZATION OF LINEAR SOLENOID VALVE AND CALIBRATION (a) Perform initialization of the linear solenoid valve and calibration    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (c) Check if the same DTCs are recorded    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result B --  REPLACE BRAKE ACTUATOR ASSEMBLY  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Hydraulic Control Assembly - Antilock Brakes/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal A --  END Steering Angle Sensor Initialization Incomplete - Electronically Controlled Brake System

dtcs.description: 66 and/or C1445/66, inspect and repair the trouble areas indicated by DTC U0073/94, U0100/65, U0123/62, U0124/95,

66 - Vehicle Driven with Steering Angle Sensor not Initialized The skid control ECU acquires steering angle sensor zero point every time the engine switch is turned on (IG) and the vehicle is driven at 35 km/h (22 mph) or more for approximately 5 seconds. The ECU also stores the previous zero point. NOTICE: * When replacing the skid control ECU, perform initialization of linear solenoid valve and calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Calibration. * Before proceeding with the inspection, explain to the customer that the output DTC is related to temporary loss of the steering angle zero point calibration, such as when the power voltage drops when the battery is removed, and confirm if this condition occurred. HINT * When DTC U0073/94, U0100/65, U0123/62, U0124/95, U0126/63, U0128/13 and/or U0130/68 is output together with DTC

63, U0128/13 and/or U0130/68 first See: U Code Charts/U0073/Electronically Controlled Brake System. * When the speed sensor or the yaw rate and acceleration sensor has trouble, DTCs for the steering angle sensor may be output even when the steering angle sensor is normal. When DTCs for the speed sensor or yaw rate and acceleration sensor are output together with other DTCs for the steering angle sensor, inspect and repair the speed sensor and yaw rate and acceleration sensor first, and then inspect and repair the steering angle sensor. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK FOR DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Turn the engine switch off. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and check that no CAN communication system DTCs are output    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (d) Drive the vehicle at a speed of 35 km/h (22 mph) and turn the steering wheel to the right and left and check that no speed sensor and yaw rate and deceleration sensor DTCs are output    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result HINT * If there is a malfunction in the speed sensor or the yaw rate and acceleration sensor, an abnormal value may be output although the steering angle sensor is normal. * If the speed sensor and the yaw rate and acceleration sensor DTCs are output simultaneously, repair the sensors and inspect the steering angle sensor. * If the same DTC C1439/66 and/or C1445/66 is not output again, the DTC may have been stored due to a temporary loss of the steering angle zero calibration, such as when the power voltage drops. B --  INSPECT CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/CAN Communication System/How to Proceed With C --  REPAIR CIRCUITS INDICATED BY OUTPUT DTCS  See: Diagnostic Trouble Code Descriptions/Electronically Controlled Brake System D --  CHECK FOR INTERMITTENT PROBLEMS  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK STEERING ANGLE SENSOR ZERO POINT CALIBRATION (a) Drive the vehicle straight ahead at 35 km/h (22 mph) or more for at least 5 seconds. (b) Check that the centered position of the steering wheel is correctly set while driving straight ahead. HINT If the front wheel alignment and steering position are adjusted as a result of an off-centered position of the steering wheel, acquire yaw rate and acceleration sensor zero point again after the adjustments are completed. OK: The centered position of the steering wheel is correctly set. NG --  ADJUST FRONT WHEEL ALIGNMENT OR STEERING POSITION OK -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Clear the DTCs    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (c) Start the engine. (d) Drive the vehicle at a speed of 35 km/h (22 mph) or more and turn the steering wheel to the right and left. (e) Check if the same DTC is output    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result HINT If the same DTC C1439/66 and/or C1445/66 is not output again, the DTC may have been stored due to a temporary loss of the steering angle zero calibration, such as when the power voltage drops. B --  END A --  REPLACE SKID CONTROL ECU  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Electronic Brake Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal Steering Angle Sensor Zero Point Malfunction - Electronically Controlled Brake System

dtcs.description: 66 - Steering Angle Sensor Zero Point Malfunction The skid control ECU acquires the steering angle sensor zero point every time the engine switch is turned on (IG) and the vehicle is driven at 35 km/h (22 mph) or more for approximately 5 seconds. The ECU also stores the previous zero point. If the front wheel alignment or steering wheel position is adjusted without disconnecting the negative battery terminal, or if the yaw rate and acceleration sensor zero point is not acquired after the adjustments have been completed, the skid control ECU detects the difference between the previous zero point and newly acquired zero point and outputs this DTC to indicate a poor adjustment. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: PERFORM ZERO POINT CALIBRATION OF YAW RATE AND ACCELERATION SENSOR (a) Perform zero point calibration of the yaw rate and acceleration sensor    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Calibration. HINT * When the stored zero point of the yaw rate and acceleration sensor is erased, the steering angle sensor zero point will also be erased. * If the zero point and output value of the yaw rate and acceleration sensor and the output value of the speed sensors are not normal, the steering angle sensor zero point cannot be acquired normally even if the vehicle is driven straight ahead at 35 km/h (22 mph) or more. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK STEERING ANGLE SENSOR ZERO POINT CALIBRATION (a) Drive the vehicle straight ahead at 35 km/h (22 mph) or more for at least 5 seconds. (b) Check that the centered position of the steering wheel is correctly set while driving straight ahead. HINT If front wheel alignment and steering position are adjusted as a result of an off-center steering wheel, acquire the yaw rate and acceleration sensor zero point again after the adjustments are completed. OK: The centered position of the steering wheel is correctly set. NG --  ADJUST FRONT WHEEL ALIGNMENT OR STEERING POSITION OK -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Clear the DTC    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (c) Check if the same DTC is recorded    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result B --  REPLACE SKID CONTROL ECU  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Electronic Brake Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal A --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK Abnormal Temperature Inside ID3 Tire - Tire Pressure Monitoring

dtcs.description: 68 - Master Reservoir Level Malfunction When a fluid level drop in the master cylinder reservoir is detected, the signal is input to the skid control ECU. When the DTC for the fluid level drop is memorized, the warning is canceled and the DTC is not stored if the fluid level returns to normal. NOTICE: When replacing the skid control ECU, perform initialization of linear solenoid valve and calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK BRAKE FLUID LEVEL IN RESERVOIR (a) Check that the brake fluid level is sufficient. OK: Brake fluid level is proper. HINT * If the fluid level drops, check for a fluid leak, and repair if found. * Check that the brake fluid leakage (Connection between accumulator and actuator, or actuator and wheel cylinder) * If no leak exists, add and adjust fluid and then check that the trouble code is not output again. (b) Check that there are no leaks from the connection between the accumulator pump and actuator. HINT As a visual check is very difficult, perform the check with the following procedures. (a) Bleed air from the brake actuator    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Brake Bleeding/Service and Repair. (b) Using the Techstream, check the data monitor of the accumulator pressure sensor. (c) Wait for 30 seconds without operating the brake pedal. Then check the accumulator pressure sensor output value's change is within the specified range. ABS/VSC/TRAC OK: Accumulator pressure sensor output value's change is within 0.2 V. NG --  CHECK AND REPAIR BRAKE FLUID LEAKAGE AND ADD FLUID OK -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT BRAKE FLUID LEVEL WARNING SWITCH (a) Disconnect the A18 switch connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. HINT A float is placed inside the reservoir. Its position can be changed by increasing/decreasing the level of brake fluid. Standard resistance: HINT If there is no problem after finishing the above check, adjust the brake fluid level to the MAX level. NG --  REPLACE BRAKE MASTER CYLINDER RESERVOIR ASSEMBLY OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (SKID CONTROL ECU - BRAKE FLUID LEVEL WARNING SWITCH) (a) Disconnect the A19 ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the A18 switch connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK SKID CONTROL ECU (SWITCH INPUT VOLTAGE) (a) Reconnect the A19 ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the A18 switch connector. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (d) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: NG --  REPLACE SKID CONTROL ECU  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Electronic Brake Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal OK -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (c) Check if the same DTC is recorded    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. HINT Reinstall the sensors, connectors, etc. and restore the vehicle to its prior condition before rechecking for DTCs. Result HINT If troubleshooting has been carried out according to the "PROBLEM SYMPTOMS TABLE", refer back to the table and proceed to the next step    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Symptom Related Diagnostic Procedures/Electronically Controlled Brake System/Problem Symptoms Table. B --  END A --  REPLACE SKID CONTROL ECU  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Electronic Brake Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal Rear Passenger Side Room Temperature Sensor Circuit - Air Conditioning System

dtcs.description: 68 - Rear Passenger Side Room Temperature Sensor Circuit The rear room temperature sensor RH is installed in the rear door trim board RH to detect the rear room temperature and control the heater and air conditioner "AUTO" mode. The resistance of the rear room temperature sensor changes in accordance with the rear room temperature for RH seat. As the temperature decreases, the resistance increases. As the temperature increases, the resistance decreases. The rear door ECU RH sends the read signal to the air conditioning amplifier via the CAN communication line. The rear door ECU RH applies voltage (5 V) to the rear room temperature sensor and reads voltage changes as the resistance of the rear room temperature sensor changes. This sensor also sends the appropriate signals to the rear door ECU RH. HINT This circuit uses the CAN communication line. Before performing the inspection, check that the CAN communication system is functioning normally. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (REAR ROOM TEMPERATURE SENSOR (for RH)) (a) Use the Data List to check if the rear room temperature sensor (for RH seat) is functioning properly. Air Conditioner OK: The display is as specified in the normal condition. Result B --  PROCEED TO NEXT CIRCUIT INSPECTION SHOWN IN PROBLEM SYMPTOMS TABLE  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Testing and Inspection/Symptom Related Diagnostic Procedures C --  REPLACE AIR CONDITIONING AMPLIFIER  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Module HVAC/Service and Repair/Removal A -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT REAR ROOM TEMPERATURE SENSOR RH (a) Remove the rear room temperature sensor RH    See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Cabin Temperature Sensor / Switch/Service and Repair/Room Temperature Sensor (for Rear Side)/Removal. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NOTICE: Even slightly touching the sensor may change the resistance value. Be sure to hold the connector of the sensor. HINT As the temperature increases, the resistance decreases (see the graph). NG --  REPLACE REAR ROOM TEMPERATURE SENSOR RH  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Cabin Temperature Sensor / Switch/Service and Repair/Room Temperature Sensor (for Rear Side)/Removal OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (REAR ROOM TEMPERATURE SENSOR RH - REAR DOOR ECU RH) (a) Disconnect the Q4 rear room temperature sensor RH connector. (b) Disconnect the O2 rear door ECU RH connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE REAR DOOR ECU RH Short in Seat Cushion Squib RH Circuit - Airbag System

dtcs.description: 68-B1883/68: Short in Seat Cushion Squib RH Circuit

68 - Short to B+ in Seat Cushion Squib RH Circuit The seat cushion squib RH circuit consists of the center airbag sensor and the seat cushion squib RH. The circuit instructs the SRS to deploy when deployment conditions are met. These DTCs are recorded when a malfunction is detected in the seat cushion squib RH circuit. NOTICE: After the engine switch is turned off, the HDD navigation system requires approximately 6 minutes to record various types of memory and settings. As a result, after turning the engine switch off, wait 6 minutes or more before disconnecting the cable from the negative (-) battery terminal. HINT * Perform the simulation method by selecting "check mode" (signal check) with the Techstream See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Check Mode Procedure. * After selecting "check mode" (signal check), perform the simulation method by wiggling each connector of the airbag system or driving the vehicle on various types of roads See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK SEAT CUSHION AIRBAG RH (SEAT CUSHION) (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Disconnect the connectors from the seat cushion airbag RH. (d) Connect the red wire side of SST (resistance: 2.1 Ohms) to connector E.

CHECK CONNECTOR (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Disconnect SST from connector E. (d) Check that the floor wire and rear seat wire connectors (on the seat cushion airbag RH side) are not damaged. OK: The lock button is not disengaged, or the claw of the lock is not damaged or deformed. NG --  REPLACE HARNESS AND CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK SEAT CUSHION SQUIB RH CIRCUIT (a) Disconnect the connectors from the center airbag sensor. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (d) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: (e) Turn the engine switch off. (f) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (g) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: (h) Release the activation prevention mechanism built into connector B    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System. (i) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: OK --  CHECK CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR NG -- Continue to next step.

CHECK REAR SEAT WIRE RH (a) Restore the released activation prevention mechanism of connector D to the original condition. (b) Disconnect the floor wire connector from the rear seat wire. (c) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (d) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (e) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: (f) Turn the engine switch off. (g) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (h) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: (i) Release the activation prevention mechanism built into connector D    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System. (j) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE REAR SEAT WIRE RH OK --  REPLACE FLOOR WIRE

CHECK CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR (a) Connect the connectors to the seat cushion airbag RH and the center airbag sensor. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. (d) Clear the DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (e) Turn the engine switch off. (f) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. (g) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. OK: DTC B1880, B1881, B1882, B1883 or 68 is not output. HINT Codes other than DTC B1880, B1881, B1882, B1883 or 68 may be output at this time, but they are not related to this check. NG --  REPLACE CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Control Module/Service and Repair/Components OK --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System Accumulator Leak Malfunction - Electronically Controlled Brake System

dtcs.description: 69 - Accumulator Leak Malfunction The DTC is stored if a brake fluid, internal or other leak is detected due to improper sealing in the brake actuator or brake accumulator pump. Internal leakage is suspected if the pump motor operates frequently without braking. NOTICE: When replacing the skid control ECU or brake actuator, perform initialization of linear solenoid valve and calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK BRAKE FLUID LEAKAGE (a) Check that there is no fluid leakage in the brake line between the brake actuator and the wheel cylinder which is the cause of DTCs. (b) Check that the brake is not dragging. OK: There is no fluid leakage or dragging. NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE APPLICABLE PART OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Turn the engine switch OFF. (c) Turn the engine switch ON (IG). (d) Check if the same DTC C1391 is recorded. Result B --  REPLACE BRAKE ACTUATOR  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Hydraulic Control Assembly - Antilock Brakes/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal A -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM ACTIVE TEST USING TECHSTREAM (SMC1, SMC2, SLRRR, AND SLRRL) (a) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (b) Turn the engine switch ON (IG). (c) Turn the Techstream main switch on. (d) Select the Active Test mode on the Techstream. HINT The Active Test can be performed when the following conditions are met. * Main relay 1 and 2 are on. * Shift lever is P position. * Parking brake is on. * Vehicle speed is 0 km/h (0 mph). ABS/VSC/TRAC (e) Using the Techstream, turn SMC1, SMC2, SLRRR, or SLRRL ON. (f) Check that each wheel cylinder's output value does not increase. OK: Each wheel cylinder's output value does not increase. HINT If any of wheel cylinder's output value increases, there may be a brake fluid leak in the brake actuator. Result B --  REPLACE BRAKE ACCUMULATOR PUMP  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Hydraulic Control Assembly - Antilock Brakes/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal A -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE BRAKE ACTUATOR Replace the brake actuator    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Hydraulic Control Assembly - Antilock Brakes/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal. NEXT --  REPLACE BRAKE ACCUMULATOR PUMP  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Hydraulic Control Assembly - Antilock Brakes/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal Abnormal Temperature Inside ID5 Tire - Tire Pressure Monitoring

dtcs.description: 69 - Infrared Ray Sensor Circuit The infrared ray sensor, which is installed on the rear roof, detects human body temperature and control the heater and air conditioner "AUTO" mode in 4 zones. Signals are transmitted to the air conditioning amplifier via the LIN communication line. This DTC is output when the temperature of the physical object is detected as -30°C (-22°F) or less, or 80°C (176°F) or more for 10 seconds or more. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: INSPECT FUSE (D-IG1-3) (a) Remove the D-IG1-3 fuse from the driver side junction block. (b) Measure the resistance of the fuse. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE FUSE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (INFRARED RAY SENSOR - BODY GROUND AND BATTERY) (a) Disconnect the X13 infrared ray sensor connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: (c) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE INFRARED RAY SENSOR  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Cabin Temperature Sensor / Switch/Service and Repair/Infrared Ray Sensor/Removal Transmitter ID not Registered - Tire Pressure Monitoring

dtcs.description: 71 - Transmitter ID not Registered The IDs of each tire pressure warning valve and transmitter are registered in the tire pressure warning ECU. When the ECU detects that a transmitter ID code is not registered in the ECU, a DTC is output. NOTICE: * When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and note them down before removal. * It is necessary to perform initialization See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization after registration See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter has been replaced. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CONFIRM REGISTRATION CONDITION (REGISTERED ID CODES) (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and the Techstream on. (d) Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Data List / (e) Check the values by referring to the table below. Tire Pressure Monitor OK: The registered transmitter ID codes are displayed on the Techstream screen. NG --  PERFORM REGISTRATION OF TRANSMITTER ID OK --  REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Module/Service and Repair/Removal

PERFORM REGISTRATION OF TRANSMITTER ID (a) Register the transmitter IDs for all the wheels    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CONFIRM TIRE INFLATION PRESSURE (DATA LIST)   See: C Code Charts/C2121 B --  END A --  REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Module/Service and Repair/Removal Electric Parking Brake High Temperature - Electric Parking Brake System

dtcs.description: 71 - Electric Parking Brake High Temperature If the electric parking brake is used continuously, the actuator overheat prevention stops the system. If this occurs, the parking brake indicator light (RED) blinks and the multi-information display shows the overheat warning. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: ELECTRIC PARKING BRAKE OVERHEAT (a) With the overheat warning displayed, wait for approximately 1 minute. (b) Check if the overheat warning has disappeared. HINT * Check the display after turning the engine switch off and on (IG). * If the display returns to normal and the electric parking brake can be operated, clear the DTC. NEXT --  END ECU Malfunction - Variable Gear Ratio Steering System

dtcs.description: 71-C15B3/73: ECU Malfunction
73 - ECU Malfunction If the steering control ECU detects an internal malfunction, it will turn the master warning light on, store these DTCs, and stop VGRS operation. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: REPLACE STEERING CONTROL ECU (a) Replace steering control ECU    See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Steering and Suspension/Relays and Modules - Steering/Steering Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal. NEXT --  END

dtcs.description: 71 - ECU Malfunction If the steering control ECU detects an internal malfunction, it will turn the master warning light on, store DTCs C15B4/71, and stop VGRS operation. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK MASTER WARNING LIGHT (a) Check the conditions under which the master warning light comes on. Result: The engine stalled, battery voltage is too low, or the steering control ECU connector is disconnected. NG --  REPLACE STEERING CONTROL ECU  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Steering and Suspension/Relays and Modules - Steering/Steering Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal OK -- Continue to next step.

CLEAR DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Variable Gear Ratio Steering System. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM VGRS SYSTEM CALIBRATION (a) Perform VGRS system calibration    See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Variable Gear Ratio Steering System/Calibration. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC (a) Check for DTCs    See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Variable Gear Ratio Steering System. Result: NG --  REPLACE STEERING CONTROL ECU  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Steering and Suspension/Relays and Modules - Steering/Steering Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal OK --  END Front Speed Sensor RH Circuit - Electronically Controlled Brake System

dtcs.description: 72 - System information not Received NOTICE: * If the electric parking brake ECU or electric parking brake actuator is replaced, perform the "Reset Memory" and "ACQUIRE TENSION SENSOR ZERO POINT" procedures See: Brakes and Traction Control/Parking Brake System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. * Before disconnecting connectors or fuses, turn the engine switch off and wait 20 seconds or more. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and the Techstream on. (d) Check for DTC    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Parking Brake System/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result: B --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting A -- Continue to next step.

CLEAR DTC (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and the Techstream on. (d) Clear the DTC    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Parking Brake System/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC (a) Drive the vehicle at 30 km/h (19 mph) or more for 5 seconds or more. (b) Turn the engine switch off. (c) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (d) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and the Techstream on. (e) Check for DTC    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Parking Brake System/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result: B --  REPLACE ELECTRIC PARKING BRAKE ECU  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Parking Brake System/Parking Brake Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal A --  GO TO CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Service Precautions/CAN Communication System ECU Malfunction - Variable Gear Ratio Steering System

73 - Open in Front Pretensioner Squib RH Circuit

(f) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. dtcs.description: 76 - Receiver Error This DTC is stored when referencing data becomes impossible due to an internal malfunction in the ECU. NOTICE: * When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and write them down before removal. * It is necessary to perform initialization See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization after registration See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter has been replaced. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CLEAR DTC (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and the Techstream on. (d) Clear the DTC    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

dtcs.description: 77 - IG Power Supply Voltage Malfunction The steering control ECU monitors IG power supply voltage. If the steering control ECU detects a malfunction, it will turn on the master warning light, store DTC C15C6/77, and stop VGRS operation. HINT The fault may be intermittent. Check harnesses and connectors thoroughly and retest. PROCEDURE

(b) Start the engine. (c) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. dtcs.description: 77 - Initialization not Completed Initialization is necessary if one of the following occurs: 1) the ECU is replaced; 2) the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter is replaced; 3) tires with a different standard tire pressure are installed; 4) the tires are rotated; or 5) a new vehicle is delivered. NOTICE: * When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and write them down before removal. * It is necessary to perform initialization See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization after registration See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter has been replaced. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK FREQUENCY RECEIVING CONDITION (a) Check for the conditions described below: (1) Facilities or devices that use similar radio frequencies are located in the vicinity of the vehicle. HINT If the vehicle is located in areas described above, the tire pressure warning light may turn on only in a particular area. (2) Devices using similar radio frequencies are used in the vehicle. OK: Facilities or devices that use similar radio frequencies are not located around or in the vicinity of the vehicle. HINT Radio frequencies may be interrupted due to the surroundings, or devices installed by the user. NG --  CHECK IF ANY DEVICE IS INSTALLED BY USER OK -- Continue to next step.

dtcs.description: 77-B1923/77: Short in Rear Pretensioner Squib RH Circuit
77 - Open in Rear Pretensioner Squib RH Circuit
77 - Short to GRD in Rear Pretensioner Squib RH Circuit

77 - Short to B+ in Rear Pretensioner Squib RH Circuit The rear pretensioner squib RH circuit consists of the center airbag sensor and the rear seat outer belt RH. This circuit instructs the SRS to deploy when deployment conditions are met. Theses DTCs are recorded when a malfunction is detected in the rear pretensioner squib RH circuit. NOTICE: After the engine switch is turned off, the HDD navigation system requires approximately 6 minutes to record various types of memory and settings. As a result, after turning the engine switch off, wait 6 minutes or more before disconnecting the cable from the negative (-) battery terminal. HINT * Perform the simulation method by selecting "check mode" (signal check) with the Techstream See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Check Mode Procedure. * After selecting "check mode" (signal check), perform the simulation method by wiggling each connector of the airbag system or driving the vehicle on various types of roads See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK REAR PRETENSIONER SQUIB RH (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Disconnect the connectors from the rear seat outer belt RH. (d) Connect the white wire side of SST (resistance: 2.1 Ohms) to connector C.

CHECK CONNECTORS (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Disconnect SST from connector C. (d) Check that the floor wire No. 2 connector (on the rear seat outer belt RH side) is not damaged. OK: The lock button is not disengaged, or the claw of the lock is not deformed or damaged. NG --  REPLACE FLOOR WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FLOOR WIRE (REAR PRETENSIONER SQUIB RH CIRCUIT) (a) Disconnect the connectors from the center airbag sensor. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (d) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: (e) Turn the engine switch off. (f) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (g) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: (h) Release the activation prevention mechanism built into connector B. (i) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE FLOOR WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR (a) Connect the connectors to the rear seat outer belt RH and the center airbag sensor. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. (d) Clear the DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (e) Turn the engine switch off. (f) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. (g) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. OK: DTC B1920, B1921, B1922, B1923 or 77 is not output. HINT Codes other than DTC B1920, B1921, B1922, B1923 and 77 may be output at this time, but they are not related to this check. NG --  REPLACE CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Control Module/Service and Repair/Components OK --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System DC Motor Power Source Voltage Malfunction - Variable Gear Ratio Steering System

dtcs.description: 78, C15C8/79: DC Motor Power Source Voltage Malfunction

79 - Power Supply Relay Failure The steering control ECU monitors PIG (motor power source voltage) and detects a malfunction in the power supply relay (located inside the steering control ECU). If the steering control ECU detects a malfunction, it will turn on the master warning light, store these DTCs, and stop VGRS operation. HINT The fault may be intermittent. Check harnesses and connectors thoroughly and retest. PROCEDURE

(d) Measure the voltage of the steering control ECU. dtcs.description: 79 - Tire Pressure Monitor ECU Communication Stop The tire pressure warning ECU exchanges speed signals and warning light signals with the combination meter via CAN communication. PROCEDURE

(d) Select "CAN Bus Check" from the System Selection Menu screen and follow the prompts on the screen to inspect the CAN Bus. NG --  GO TO CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/CAN Communication System/Diagnosis System OK -- Continue to next step.

Result B --  GO TO CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/CAN Communication System/Diagnosis System A -- Continue to next step.

dtcs.description: 81 or B1628/82 is recorded when a malfunction is detected in the circuit for the side collision sensor LH or RH (to determine deployment of the front seat side airbag LH or RH and curtain shield airbag LH or RH).

82 - Side Satellite Sensor Bus LH Initialization Incomplete The circuit for the side collision sensor LH or RH (to determine deployment of the front seat side airbag LH or RH and curtain shield airbag LH or RH) is composed of the center airbag sensor, side airbag sensor LH or RH, and rear airbag sensor LH or RH. The side airbag sensor LH or RH and rear airbag sensor LH or RH detect impacts to the vehicle and send signals to the center airbag sensor to determine if the airbag should be deployed.

81 or B1638/82 is recorded when a malfunction is detected in the circuit for the rear collision sensor LH or RH (to determine deployment of the rear seat side airbag LH or RH and curtain shield airbag LH or RH).

81 or B1648/82 is recorded when a malfunction is detected in the circuit for the rear collision sensor LH or RH (to determine deployment of the front and rear seat side airbag LH or RH and curtain shield airbag LH or RH). NOTICE: After the engine switch is turned off, the HDD navigation system requires approximately 6 minutes to record various types of memory and settings. As a result, after turning the engine switch off, wait 6 minutes or more before disconnecting the cable from the negative (-) battery terminal. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK PAST DTC (a) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. (b) Turn the engine switch off. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. (d) Check the past DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result: A --  GO TO DTC B1622  See: Diagnostic Trouble Code Descriptions/Airbag System B --  GO TO DTC B1632  See: B Code Charts/B1632 C -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR DTC (a) Turn the engine switch off. HINT If a communication error occurs, DTCs for both the LH and RH sides will be stored simultaneously. To identify the malfunctioning area, turn the engine switch off and then on (IG) again. (b) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. (c) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result: A --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System B --  CHECK CONNECTION OF CONNECTORS C -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CONNECTION OF CONNECTORS (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Check that the connectors are properly connected to the center airbag sensor and side airbag sensor RH. OK: The connectors are properly connected. NG --  CONNECT CONNECTORS PROPERLY OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CONNECTORS (a) Disconnect the connectors from the center airbag sensor and side airbag sensor RH. (b) Check that the connectors (on the center airbag sensor side and side airbag sensor RH side) are not damaged. OK: The connectors are not deformed or damaged. dtcs.description: 81 or B1637/82 is recorded when a malfunction is detected in the circuit for the side collision sensor LH or RH (to determine deployment of the front seat side airbag LH or RH and curtain shield airbag LH or RH).

82 - Lost Communication with Side Satellite Sensor Bus LH The circuit for the side collision sensor LH or RH (to determine deployment of the front seat side airbag LH or RH and curtain shield airbag LH or RH) is composed of the center airbag sensor, side airbag sensor LH or RH, and rear airbag sensor LH or RH. The side airbag sensor LH or RH and rear airbag sensor LH or RH detect impacts to the vehicle and send signals to the center airbag sensor to determine if the airbag should be deployed.

81 or B1647/82 is recorded when a malfunction is detected in the circuit for the side collision sensor LH or RH (to determine deployment of the front and rear seat side airbag LH or RH and curtain shield airbag LH or RH). NOTICE: After the engine switch is turned off, the HDD navigation system requires approximately 6 minutes to record various types of memory and settings. As a result, after turning the engine switch off, wait 6 minutes or more before disconnecting the cable from the negative (-) battery terminal. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 83 - Lost Communication with Front Airbag Sensor RH

83 - Front Airbag Sensor RH Initialization Incomplete The front airbag sensor RH circuit consists of the center airbag sensor and front airbag sensor RH. dtcs.description: 84 - Lost Communication with Front Airbag Sensor LH

84 - Front Airbag Sensor LH Initialization Incomplete The front airbag sensor LH circuit consists of the center airbag sensor and front airbag sensor LH. The front airbag sensor LH detects impacts to the vehicle and sends signals to the center airbag sensor to determine if the airbag should be deployed.

86 - Driver Side Air Outlet Damper Control Servo Motor Circuit The mode damper servo (for driver side front A/C rear air flow) sends pulse signals to inform the air conditioning amplifier of the damper position. The air conditioning amplifier activates the motor (normal, reverse) based on the signals to move the mode damper servo (for driver side front A/C rear air flow) to any position, which controls the air outlet changes. dtcs.description: 89 - Rear Air Outlet Damper Control Servo Motor RH Circuit The rear outlet damper servo (for RH side) sends pulse signals to inform the air conditioning amplifier of the damper position. The air conditioning amplifier activates the motor (normal or reverse) based on these signals to move the rear outlet damper (for RH side) to the appropriate position, which controls the air outlet modes. NOTICE: * If this DTC is output, perform the following procedures before beginning the INSPECTION PROCEDURE. (1) Change the air outlet mode position for the rear LH and RH seats to "FOOT". (2) Turn the engine switch off, and wait approximately 90 seconds. (3) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (4) Clear DTCs See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. (5) With the air outlet mode position for the rear LH and RH seats on "FOOT", wait approximately 45 seconds. (6) Check for DTCs See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. If this DTC is not output, there is no malfunction. If this DTC is output, perform the INSPECTION PROCEDURE below. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 91 - Vehicle Speed Signal Error (Test Mode DTC) The tire pressure warning ECU receives speed signals from the combination meter via CAN communication. This DTC is stored upon entering test mode, and cleared when a vehicle speed signal of 20 km/h (12 mph) is detected for 3 seconds or more. This DTC is output only in test mode. NOTICE: * When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and write them down before removal. * It is necessary to perform initialization See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization after registration See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter has been replaced. PROCEDURE s/CAN Communication System OK -- Continue to next step. s/Electronically Controlled Brake System OK -- Continue to next step. s/Meter / Gauge System OK -- Continue to next step.

steps.instruction: CHECK CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and the Techstream on. (d) Select "CAN Bus Check" from the System Selection Menu screen, and follow the prompts on the screen, to inspect the CAN Bus. NG --  GO TO CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM (HOW TO PROCEED WITH TROUBLE SHOOTING)  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/CAN Communication System/How to Proceed With Troubleshooting OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC (CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM) (a) Check for DTC    See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/CAN Communication System/Diagnosis System. Result NG --  GO TO CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM (DIAGNOSTIC TROUBLE CODE CHART)  See: Diagnostic Trouble Code

CHECK DTC (BRAKE CONTROL SYSTEM) (a) Check if DTCs for the brake control system are output    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. OK: No DTCs for the brake control system are output. NG --  GO TO BRAKE CONTROL SYSTEM (DIAGNOSTIC TROUBLE CODE CHART)  See: Diagnostic Trouble Code

CHECK DTC (METER / GAUGE SYSTEM) (a) Check if DTCs for the meter / gauge system are output    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. OK: No DTCs for the meter / gauge system are output. NG --  GO TO METER / GAUGE SYSTEM (DIAGNOSTIC TROUBLE CODE CHART)  See: Diagnostic Trouble Code

CHECK VEHICLE SPEED SIGNAL (DATA LIST) (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and the Techstream on. (d) Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Data List / (e) Check that the values indicated on the Techstream and on the combination meter are the same. Tire Pressure Monitor OK: The vehicle speed displayed on the Techstream changes depending on the actual vehicle speed. NG --  REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Module/Service and Repair/Removal OK --  GO TO TEST MODE PROCEDURE  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Test Mode Procedure Infrared Ray Sensor Communication Malfunction - Air Conditioning System

dtcs.description: 92 - Infrared Ray Sensor Communication Malfunction This circuit consists of the infrared ray sensor and the air conditioning amplifier. The infrared ray sensor, which is installed on the rear roof, detects human body temperature and controls the heater and air conditioner "AUTO" mode in 4 zone. Signals are transmitted to the air conditioning amplifier via LIN communication line. This DTC is output when LIN communication between the infrared ray sensor and air conditioning amplifier stop for 10 seconds or more. If the LIN communication system malfunctions, the air conditioning amplifier does not operate even if the infrared ray sensor is normal. HINT For vehicles with infrared ray sensor only. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (INFRARED RAY SENSOR - AIR CONDITIONING AMPLIFIER) (a) Disconnect the X13 infrared ray sensor connector. (b) Disconnect the L76 air conditioning amplifier connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE INFRARED RAY SENSOR (a) Replace the infrared ray sensor with a new or properly functioning one    See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Cabin Temperature Sensor / Switch/Service and Repair/Infrared Ray Sensor/Removal. (b) Operate the infrared ray sensor to check that it functions properly. OK: Infrared ray sensor function operates normally. Result B --  END (INFRARED RAY SENSOR IS FAULTY) A --  REPLACE AIR CONDITIONING AMPLIFIER  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Module HVAC/Service and Repair/Removal Infrared Ray Sensor Communication Malfunction - LIN Communication System

dtcs.description: 93 - Max Hot Bypass Damper COOL Control Circuit The max hot bypass damper servo motor (for COOL) sends pulse signals to inform the air conditioning amplifier of the damper position. The air conditioning amplifier activates the motor (normal, reverse) based on the signals to move the max hot bypass damper servo motor (for COOL) to any position. As a result, when the air conditioner is operating, the temperature of the air blowing toward the driver's head area is controlled. HINT Confirm that no mechanical problem is present because this diagnostic code can be output when either a damper link or damper is mechanically locked. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 98 - Steering Angle Sensor Unusual Bank Angle Detected The skid control ECU receives signals from the yaw rate and acceleration sensor via the CAN communication system. The yaw rate sensor has a built-in acceleration sensor and detects the vehicle condition. If there is trouble in the bus lines between the yaw rate and acceleration sensor and the CAN communication system, DTCs U0123/62 (malfunction in CAN communication with the yaw rate sensor) and U0124/95 (malfunction in CAN communication with the acceleration sensor) are output. NOTICE: * When replacing the skid control ECU, perform initialization of linear solenoid valve and calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Calibration. * Before proceeding with the inspection, explain to the customer that the vehicle driving record shows that the vehicle was driven on a bank at an angle of 30°, and confirm if this condition occurred. HINT When DTC U0123/62, U0124/95 and/or U126/63 is output together with DTC C1440/98, inspect and repair the trouble areas indicated by DTC U0123/62, U0124/95 and/or U0126/63 first See: U Code Charts/U0073/Electronically Controlled Brake System. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK FOR DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Turn the engine switch off. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and check that no CAN communication system DTCs are output    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (d) Drive the vehicle at a speed of 30 km/h (19 mph) or more, turn the steering wheel, and decelerate (depress the brake pedal) the vehicle and check that no yaw rate and acceleration sensor DTCs are output    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result HINT

OK: The sensor is tightened to the specified torque. The sensor is not installed in a tilted position.
